Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 22 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥いらっしゃいませんね。 おヒゲのけいじさん。
English: It doesn't look like Mr. Scruffy Detective is here.

Japanese: カメラを持って、電気屋を まわってくれているんだよ。
English: Well, he's out there with that camera asking around at all the electronic stores.

Japanese: じゃあわたくし、お夜食に のりまきをお作りしますね。
English: Then, I'll make some salad for him for dinner.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん‥‥イトノコ刑事に カンシャしてるみたいだな）
English: (It looks like Pearls really appreciates what Gumshoe is doing for us...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの、なるほどくん。
English: ...Um, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ん？　なに。
English: Hm? Yes?

Japanese: かんぴょうはどこですか？
English: Where is the lettuce?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなの、 買ったことないよ。
English: ...I don't think I've ever bought lettuce before.

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥ のりまきはあきらめましょう。
English: Aww, I guess I have to give up on making a salad, then.

Japanese: （何やらコダワリが あるらしいな‥‥）
English: (Guess the lack of lettuce is kind of a problem.)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。わたくし、 お水をあげますね！
English: Oh! I'll water it!

Japanese: い、いや。さっき イトノコ刑事がジャバジャバと‥‥
English: Ah, it's OK... Gumshoe already gave it more than plenty earlier...

Japanese: ‥‥じょうろ、じょうろ‥‥
English: ...The watering can... Where are you, Mr. Watering Can...?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、何かしてないと 不安なんだ‥‥）
English: (Pearls gets too wound up if she's not doing something to distract herself...)

Japanese: （チャーリーくんには、 カクゴしてもらうしかないな）
English: (I hope Charley can withstand Typhoon Pearls...)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。何かホーリツをお調べに なるのですか！
English: Oh! Do you need to look something up in one of these law books, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ど、どの本が！ どの本がヒツヨウなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Which book is it!? Which book do you need!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥漢字が読めないし、 背もとどきません‥‥
English: Umm... I can't read those hard books, and... um... I can't reach them either...

Japanese: い、いいよ。 気をつかわなくて。
English: I-It's OK, Pearls. Really.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちなみに、事件があったホテルは 最近、街の中心に完成した、
English: Actually, the crime happened at the recently completed hotel in the center of town,

Japanese: ホテル・バンドー・インペリアル。
English: the Gatewater Imperial Hotel.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どこまで デカくなっていくのやら‥‥。
English: ...Just how far are they going to expand their empire...?

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: あ。もっとピカピカに みがきましょう！
English: Oh, I'll wipe it some more and make it super shiny!

Japanese: ‥‥おふきん、おふきん‥‥
English: ...The wiping cloth... Where are you, Mr. wiping cloth...?

Japanese: いいよ。これ以上みがいたら、 まぶしくて座れないから。
English: It's fine. Really. If you wipe it any more, it'll be so shiny I'll be using it as a mirror.

Japanese: これは‥‥とのさまん・ほい ‥‥ですね。
English: This is the... uh... Nickel Samurai, right?

Japanese: う、うん。《ほい》だね。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 真宵さまの《ほい》に なってくださいね！
English: Please take care of Mystic Maya, and be her "Nickel Samurai", alright!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 22 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: なるほどくん！　お電話が‥‥
English: Mr. Nick! Your phone!

Japanese: こうして聞いてみると‥‥
English: I don't like the sound of this ringtone right now...

Japanese: ‥‥なんて不吉な メロディなんだろう。
English: It sounds kind of ominous.

Japanese: 気のせいだと思いますけど。
English: It's probably just your imagination.

Japanese: ‥‥それより、 早く出てくださいっ！
English: You should really pick it up, Mr. Nick! It could be important!

Japanese: もしもし
English: Hello?

Japanese: タイヘンッス！
English: We've got a big problem, pal!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか
English: Wh-What's wrong??

Japanese: とにかく、 すぐ事務所にもどるッス！
English: I'm on my way to your office right now!

Japanese: わ、わかりました！
English: O-OK!

Japanese: （‥‥って、待てよ。 “事務所に”‥‥？）
English: (...Hey, wait a sec. Why is he coming here?)

Japanese: もう、何がなんだかわかんねッス！ ダメッス！　終わりッス！自分は
English: It's terrible! I don't know what to say, pal! It's the end, I say. I'm going to...

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 ずいぶん早かったッスね‥‥。
English: Oh. I got here faster than I thought...

Japanese: ど、どうも。
English: Um... H-Hey...

Japanese: 落ちついてる場合じゃないッス！ ホレ、もっとあわてるッス！
English: No time to relax now, pal! I'm confused as anything here!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 22 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: おそいッス！　御剣検事、 もう帰っちまったッス！
English: Hey, pal! Mr. Edgeworth's already back!

Japanese: いったい‥‥どうしたんですか？
English: Well? What happened?

Japanese: わかったッス！ 盗撮カメラをしかけた人物が！
English: We got 'em! We know who bought that spy camera!

Japanese: ええっ！
English: Eh!?

Japanese: こ‥‥こんなに早く？
English: Th-This quickly?

Japanese: キメテは、このクマだったッス！
English: And this bear's what gave them away, pal!

Japanese: クマ‥‥
English: The bear...

Japanese: 自分は気づいたッス！
English: I figured it out, pal!

Japanese: カメラやトランスミッターより、 このクマに注目すべきだ、と！
English: I figured that we should've been looking into the bear instead of the camera!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 それは、みつるぎ検事が‥‥
English: Um... Wasn't that Mr. Edgeworth that figured...

Japanese: しっ！　‥‥それで？
English: Shh, Pearls. ...And? Go on.

Japanese: 事件の関係者が１人だけ、 買っていたッス！　コイツを！
English: There's only one person who bought one of those bears who's related to this crime!

Japanese: いったい‥‥ どなたなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Who is it!?

Japanese: ヒトさまの部屋をのぞこう などと言う、フラチな‥‥
English: Who would be so rude as to spy on another person in their room...?

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼　真悟ッス。
English: ...Matt Engarde.
Huh?

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟。 アンタたちの依頼人ッス！
English: Matt Engarde. Your client, that's who, pal!

Japanese: （‥‥サイアクだ‥‥）
English: (And here I thought things couldn't get any worse...)

Japanese: ‥‥アンタが認めたくない キモチは、よくわかるッス。
English: I know you don't want to give up, pal...

Japanese: （まさか‥‥とは思っていた）
English: (I never thought... I didn't think it was possible...)

Japanese: （イサオさんの部屋を盗撮して いたのは‥‥オートロさん！）
English: (The person who put the spy camera in Juan Corrida's room was Matt Engarde!)

Japanese: どうして‥‥オートロさんは そんなことを！
English: Why... Why would Mr. Engarde do something like this!?

Japanese: そりゃァ、華宮　霧緒との密会を 撮影しようとしたッスよ。きっと。
English: I bet it was to catch Ms. Andrews and Mr. Corrida in one of their rendezvous.

Japanese: “きっと”じゃダメなんです。
English: "I bet" is not good enough for me.

Japanese: ぼくは、本当のコトを 知らなければならない。
English: I have to know the absolute truth behind this camera.

Japanese: ‥‥会うッスか？　王都楼と。
English: Are you going to see him? Mr. Engarde, I mean.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥こわいです、なるほどくん。
English: I'm... I'm scared, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: いったい‥‥何が 飛び出してくるんでしょう‥‥
English: I wonder... I wonder what we will find out next...

Japanese: （ぼくもコワいけど‥‥ 言えるフンイキじゃないな）
English: (I'm scared myself, but I have to put on a good face for Pearls...)

Japanese: （オートロさん‥‥あなたは いったい‥‥何をしたんだ！）
English: (Matt Engarde! What in the world have you done!?)

Japanese: ‥‥おッ。ちょっと しおれてるッスね！
English: Hey, it's wilting a little!

Japanese: 自分が‥‥自分が、 おいしい水をおごるッス！
English: I'll... I'll give it some delicious water, pal!

Japanese: い、いや。今日は もう、あげましたから‥‥
English: Ah, it's OK... I already did that...

Japanese: ‥‥じょうろ、じょうろ‥‥
English: ...The watering can... Where are you, Mr. Watering Can...?

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくのゴキゲンをとって どうするつもりだよ‥‥）
English: (Did he just call the watering can, "Mr."?)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。何かホーリツをお調べに なるのですか！
English: Oh! Do you need to look something up in one of these law books, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: ど、どの本が！ どの本がヒツヨウなのですかッ！
English: Wh-Which book is it!? Which book do you need!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥漢字が読めないし、 背もとどきません‥‥
English: Umm... I can't read those hard books, and... um... I can't reach them either...

Japanese: じ、自分なら！　漢字も読めるし 背もとどくッス！
English: B-But I can read AND I'm tall, pal!

Japanese: は、春美ちゃんと 張り合わないでくださいよ。
English: P-Please stop competing with an 8 year old... It's disturbing...

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちなみに、事件があったホテルは 最近、街の中心に完成した、
English: Actually, the crime happened at the recently completed hotel in the center of town,

Japanese: ホテル・バンドー・インペリアル。
English: the Gatewater Imperial Hotel.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どこまで デカくなっていくのやら‥‥。
English: ...Just how far are they going to expand their empire...?

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: あ。わたくしがゆうべ、 さらにキチンとかたづけました！
English: Oh! I cleaned it up even more for you last night!

Japanese: じ、自分が‥‥さらに ゾーキンがけしとくッス！
English: B-But I can get it even more cleaner, pal!

Japanese: きゃあ！　それは おトイレ用の‥‥！
English: Aaah! That's... That's for the toilet!!

Japanese: （‥‥おおさわぎだな‥‥）
English: (What a mess. Literally...)

Japanese: これは‥‥とのさまん・ほい ‥‥ですね。
English: This is the... uh... Nickel Samurai, right?

Japanese: う、うん。《ほい》だね。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 真宵さまの《ほい》に なってくださいね！
English: Please take care of Mystic Maya, and be her "Nickel Samurai", alright!?

Japanese: まちがいないんですか！ このぬいぐるみを買った人物‥‥
English: Are you sure you heard right!? That the person who bought this bear was...

Japanese: デパートの店員に会って来たッス。
English: I heard it from the department store clerk, pal.

Japanese: コイツは、クレジットカードの 伝票ッス。
English: This is the credit card receipt for the purchase.

Japanese: ‥‥３８万円。あのぬいぐるみの 値段と、ピッタリ一致するッス。
English: It's for $3,800, pal. That's an exact match to the price of that stuffed bear.
A receipt...? That's all you have?

Japanese: もちろん、伝票だけじゃないッス。 店員の証言があるッス。
English: Nah, it's not just the receipt, pal. The store clerk said so himself.

Japanese: “まちがいなく王都楼に売った” ‥‥そう証言してるッス。
English: He told me, "I'm sure I sold the bear to Mr. Engarde."

Japanese: ついでに、その店員はサインも もらったらしいッス。
English: I mean, the clerk even got Mr. Engarde's autograph out of it, pal.

Japanese: ぬいぐるみを買ったのは‥‥ たしかに、王都楼ッス！
English: So I'm sure the person that bought the stuffed bear was Mr. Engarde himself!

Japanese: （‥‥め、目まいが‥‥）
English: (My... My sight is failing me... Th-This can't be!)

Japanese: 証拠品《クレジットカードの伝票》 のデータをファイルした。
English: Credit Card Receipt added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ビデオカメラのほうは どうでしたか‥‥？
English: So what about the spy camera we found...?

Japanese: コイツは、調べてもムダッス。 どこでも入手できるッスからね。
English: Ah, that was a dead end, pal. I mean, you can get this kind of thing from anywhere.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、あずかった 証拠は返しておくッス。
English: But for now, I guess I can give these back to you for you to file away into evidence.

Japanese: あずけていた証拠品のデータを もう一度、ファイルした。
English: Spy Camera, Transmitter, and Stuffed Bear refiled into the Court Record.

Japanese: ほら、弁護士バッジですよ。
English: Look, it's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: おッ！　ちょっと くもってるッスね。
English: Oh! It's a little tarnished, don't you think, pal?

Japanese: 自分が、ピカピカに みがいてあげるッス！
English: Don't worry! I'll make it all sparkly shiny again!

Japanese: （なんか、 ナミダぐましいなあ‥‥）
English: (I... I'm really touched...)

Japanese: ユーカイ犯から、連絡は‥‥？
English: Have you heard anything from the kidnapper...?

Japanese: 電波障害で切れちゃったきり、 連絡がありません‥‥。
English: Nope. Not a word since we were cut off by the radio interference.

Japanese: ヤツの目的がオートロの 無罪判決であるかぎり‥‥
English: Well, we know his goal is to get an acquittal for Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 人質に危害をくわえることは できないはずッス。
English: So, I don't think he'll hurt Maya, because that's not going to help him at all.

Japanese: キボーを捨てちゃ ダメッスよ！
English: Come on! We can't lose hope, pal!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: 自分はそれを見たとき、
English: When I saw that button,

Japanese: “これで王都楼の有罪は決定だ！” と思ったッスが‥‥
English: I thought to myself, "This is gonna prove Mr. Engarde's guilty!"...

Japanese: いやー、捜査はオクが深いッス。
English: But it looks like there was a lot more to it than I thought, huh pal?

Japanese: プロがそんなこと言っちゃ ダメでしょう。
English: You're a pro. You should know better than to say something like that.

Japanese: ま。ま。自分はもう、 事務員ッスから。
English: W-Well, I'm not a cop anymore. Just a regular helper around the office now, so...

Japanese: （刑事にミレンはないのか このヒト‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if he misses working down at the precinct...?)

Japanese: まさかこれで、王都楼が ステーキを食べていたとは‥‥
English: Who would've thought that this knife Mr. Engarde used to eat his dinner steak with...

Japanese: ‥‥ごくフツーの ハナシだと思いますけど。
English: Go on...

Japanese: これを死体に刺したの‥‥ 華宮　霧緒だったッス。
English: ...That it would be stabbed into the victim's body by Ms. Andrews, of all people.

Japanese: そこまで被害者のコト、 キライだったッスかねえ。
English: I guess she just hated the victim that much, huh pal?

Japanese: それより、問題は‥‥
English: The question isn't why she hated the victim to that extent, but rather

Japanese: そこまでして、王都楼さんに 罪を着せようとしたことです。
English: why she tried to pin the murder on Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: おキライだったんでしょうか？ オートロさんのこと‥‥
English: I wonder if she really hates Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: このトノサマンも 華宮　霧緒だったッスか‥‥
English: So this "Nickel Samurai" was really Ms. Andrews, huh?

Japanese: ええ。ナイフを取りに もどったところか‥‥
English: Yeah. This is either when she went to Mr. Engarde's room to get the knife...

Japanese: すべてのシゴトを終えたところか ‥‥どっちかでしょうね。
English: ...or when she was done with the foul deed. But I guess we'll never know.

Japanese: あ、そうだ。 このカード、知ってますか？
English: Oh yeah! Do you know about this card?
Card?

Japanese: 御剣のヤツは、これを見て カオ色を変えてましたけど。
English: Edgeworth, for some reason, went pale the instant he saw this card.

Japanese: そりゃァ、知ってるッス。
English: Hey, I know what this is, pal!

Japanese: え！
English: You do?

Japanese: こりゃアンタ、どう見ても サザエッス。
English: No matter what way you look at it, I'd say it's a picture of a shell.

Japanese: あの。それだけですか？
English: Um, that's it?

Japanese: あ、そうそう！
English: Oh yeah! That's right!

Japanese: ちなみに御剣検事は、サザエの ツボ焼きが大好きッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth really likes those cooked snail things.

Japanese: あ、イヤ。あれは、なんとかいう カタツムリだったッスっけ‥‥？
English: Um, what are they called again...? "Escargot" or something like that...?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 検事さんのカオ色が変わったナゾ、 解けましたね！
English: I think we just solved the mystery of why Mr. Edgeworth's face turned pale, right!?

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、知らされてない のか‥‥この刑事さんには）
English: (As I suspected, Gumshoe has no clue...)

Japanese: オートロのヤツ‥‥ 何を考えてるッスかねえ。
English: That Engarde... What was he thinking?

Japanese: 被害者の部屋にカメラを しかけるなんて‥‥
English: You mean setting the spy camera up in the victim's room...?

Japanese: しかも、殺人の瞬間を ビデオに記録していたッス。
English: Yeah! And it was recording at the time of the murder too, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥はたして、グーゼン だったッスかねえ‥‥
English: Don't you find that just a tad suspicious? It doesn't sound like a coincidence to me...

Japanese: あ、イヤ！　ベツに自分は そういうイミで言ったのでは‥‥
English: Ah! I mean, don't mind me! I didn't mean it that way, pal...

Japanese: （“そういうイミ”って‥‥ どういうイミだ？）
English: (Then what "way" did you mean it?)

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥
English: Could you please take a look at this...?

Japanese: あー‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: 特に、何も 思い浮かばないッス。
English: I can't think of anything to say about this, pal.

Japanese: お、おわびに ソーメン、ゴチソウするッス。
English: Wh-Why don't I make us some instant noodles, instead?

Japanese: い、いいですよベツに。
English: Th-That's alright... Really.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトもうらやむ “刑事ヅラ”だったッス。
English: People are jealous of my roguishly cool "detective look", pal.

Japanese: このカオを買われて、刑事の テストにも合格したッス。
English: It's because of this look that I passed the detective's test, you know.

Japanese: え。
English: Oh.

Japanese: でも、やっぱり‥‥カオだけじゃあ ダメッスね、ニンゲンは。
English: But you know, people can't coast through life on their looks alone, pal.

Japanese: ダメだって。カオだけじゃ。
English: Yeah, you can't count on your looks alone.

Japanese: はああ‥‥ベンキョウに なりますねえ‥‥。
English: Wow... I learned something new today...

Japanese: ゲンキ出すッスよ。 負けちゃダメッス。
English: Cheer up! You can't give in, missy!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: あ、そうだ！
English: Hey! I know!

Japanese: 今、イイもの見せてあげるッス。 ホンモノのピストルを‥‥。
English: I'll show you something cool! How's this? It's a real, genuine pistol...

Japanese: ピストルも警察手帳も ‥‥もう持ってなかったッス。
English: I... I don't have my pistol or my police badge anymore...

Japanese: ゲンキ出してください！ 負けてはダメです！
English: Cheer up! You can't give in!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: （ナニやってんだよ‥‥）
English: (What are those two doing...?)

Japanese: あんな御剣検事は はじめてッス。
English: That's the first time I've seen that side of Mr. Edgeworth, pal.

Japanese: 思いどおりに証言させるため、 ナリフリかまわない‥‥
English: Making people say what he wants them to during testimony...

Japanese: いったい、ナニが あったッスかねえ‥‥
English: I want to know what in the world happened to him all this time he's been gone...

Japanese: だいじょうぶッス！ きっと、また会えるッス！
English: It'll be OK! You'll see her again, little missy!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: あきらめちゃダメッスよ！ それがカンジンッス！
English: It's really important that you don't give up!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: O-OK!

Japanese: （やっぱり、声がデカいと 説得力があるな‥‥）
English: (I guess a big voice really does give you a sense of presence...)

Japanese: わたくし‥‥ あきらめませんとも！
English: I... I won't give up. Ever!

Japanese: 狙撃‥‥されたんですよね。 だいじょうぶなんですか？
English: Is she alright...? After being shot, I mean...

Japanese: 彼女のキズは、浅いッス。 ‥‥撃たれたワリには。
English: Her wound isn't that bad... Well, the gunshot wound anyway...

Japanese: でも‥‥ココロに受けた キズは、そうとう深いッス。
English: But the wound to her pride, now that's a different story, pal.

Japanese: ココロに‥‥？
English: The wound to her pride?

Japanese: まあ、今まで守ってきた “狩魔”のカンバンが、
English: Well, until now, she's always upheld the "Von Karma Creed",

Japanese: ハデにぶっ倒されたわけッス からねえ‥‥アンタに。
English: but since she came here, well, you've given her pride quite a beating, pal.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: なんと言っても、彼女はまだ １８才の女の子ッス。
English: I mean, she may act all grown- up, but she's really still just an 18 year old.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、シンパイッス。
English: ...Quite frankly, I worry about her, pal.

Japanese: 自分はあまり 活劇にはくわしくないッスが‥‥
English: I'm not a real fan of action shows or anything...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオといえば、 さすがに知ってるッス。
English: But I know who Juan Corrida and Matt Engarde are.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の人気をうばおうと 必死だったッス。
English: Juan Corrida, though, he was always desperately trying to be better than Matt Engarde.

Japanese: この男のために、真宵さんを ユーカイするなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe it. Maya getting kidnapped all because of this guy...

Japanese: いったい、ハンニンは どんなヤツッスかねえ！
English: I mean, what kind of person is the real murderer anyway!?

Japanese: やっぱり、オートロさんの 大ファンのかた、でしょうか？
English: Maybe they're a big fan of Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: （‥‥あの、誘拐犯の声‥‥）
English: (But the kidnapper's voice...)

Japanese: （なんていうか、 おじいさん‥‥に聞こえたけど）
English: (He sounded like an old man to me...)

Japanese: 彼女のイメージが、 だいぶ変わったッス。
English: My impression of her has totally changed, pal.

Japanese: あの‥‥彼女は、いま‥‥？
English: Hmm... I wonder what she is doing right now...?

Japanese: 警察で事情聴取されてる はずッスよ。
English: I'm sure she's still being questioned.

Japanese: おそらく、死体の破損や 捜査をカクランした容疑で、
English: She sure got herself into quite a big mess trying to frame Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 留置所に入れられることに なるのでは‥‥
English: She'll probably be spending the night at the detention center...

Japanese: （留置所、か‥‥）
English: (Yeah, that's right. She's still at the detention center.)

Japanese: 彼女は、華宮　霧緒の 先輩だったッス。
English: She was Ms. Adrian Andrews' mentor.

Japanese: （たよりきっていた先輩が 自殺してしまって‥‥）
English: (Because of the suicide of the mentor she depended on,)

Japanese: （華宮　霧緒は、あとを追って 自分も死のうとした‥‥）
English: (Adrian Andrews tried to follow her in an attempted suicide...)

Japanese: （じゃあ、今の彼女は だれに“依存”を‥‥？）
English: (Now the question is, who is Ms. Andrews relying on now...?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥面会時間は とっくに終わっている。
English: Visiting hours ended a few hours ago.

Japanese: もう、オートロさんに ハナシを聞くのはムリか‥‥。
English: Looks like we're not going to get a chance to talk with Mr. Engarde tonight...

Japanese: で、でも‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: 大事なお話があるのでは‥‥？
English: Isn't what we have to ask very important?

Japanese: （そうなんだよ。 なんとかならないかな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, but I don't think that matters to the guard...)

Japanese: 面会時間が終わったのに、 何を見張っているんだろう。
English: Visiting hours ended hours ago, so what's he watching over now?

Japanese: 何かイタズラでもなさって、 バツとして立たされているのでは？
English: Maybe he did something bad and he's standing there as punishment.

Japanese: （ま、まさかな‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I don't think that's it...)

Japanese: 面会時間が終わったのに、 何を見張っているんだろう。
English: Visiting hours ended hours ago, so what's the camera watching over now?

Japanese: あそこに立たされている おまわりさんではないでしょうか？
English: Maybe it's watching the Mr. Policeman who's standing over there.

Japanese: （ま、まさかな‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I don't think that's it...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （今、この留置所には‥‥）
English: (I'm sure they must have transferred Ms. Andrews here by now...)

Japanese: （オートロさんの他に、 霧緒さんもいるはずだ）
English: (So that means that both Mr. Engarde and Ms. Andrews are in this detention center.)

Japanese: （‥‥さて。どちらの話を 聞かせてもらおうかな？）
English: (Now then, whose story do I want to hear?)

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありませんが‥‥
English: I'm really sorry, but...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は、あなたとの 面会を拒否しています。
English: Matt Engarde is refusing to meet with you.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: （まあ、いいだろう。 もう、話すべきことはない）
English: (Well, I guess I don't really have anything left to talk to him about anyway...)

Japanese: それでは、霧緒さんの お話を聞きましょうか‥‥。
English: Shall we listen to what Ms. Andrews has to say...?

Japanese: あっ、成歩堂さん！
English: Dude! It's Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 明日こそは、ボクの 無実を証明してくれますよね！
English: I hope you can get me off the hook tomorrow. I'm counting on you!

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥。
English: I... hope so too...

Japanese: （御剣は言った）
English: (Edgeworth just dropped a bombshell on me and said...)

Japanese: （イサオさんを殺害したのは 殺し屋で、その依頼人は‥‥）
English: (that Juan Corrida was killed by an assassin, and that assassin's client is...)

Japanese: （この、王都楼　真悟だ、と‥‥）
English: (this man... Matt Engarde...)

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥オートロさん。
English: ...Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: どうしても知っておきたい ことがあるんですよ。
English: There is something I must know with one hundred percent certainty.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、弁護士さん。 いつもとちがいますね。
English: Hmm, you seem kinda different. You're totally not like your usual lawyer dude self.

Japanese: ごくろうさまですね、成歩堂さん。 もう、１０時すぎですよ。
English: You're working really late, you know. It's already past 10:00 PM, dude.

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、ハッキリした 答えを出したいと思ったんです。
English: I think it's time you told me the truth.

Japanese: 答えを知らないほうが シアワセなこともありますけどね。
English: Relax. Don't you know that "ignorance is bliss"?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、お話を聞きましょうか。
English: But if you really want to know, let's talk.

Japanese: どうした？　弁護士クン。 さっきから、ダマりこんで‥‥
English: What's wrong, Mister Lawyer? You've grown awfully quiet...

Japanese: ぼくは‥‥ ダマされていたのか？
English: H-How could I have been so deceived by you all this time?

Japanese: 初めて会ったとき、きみに聞いた。 “イサオさんを殺害したか”‥‥？
English: When we first met, I asked if you had killed Juan Corrida...

Japanese: きみは、ハッキリ答えた。 “だれも殺していない”と。
English: And you answered very clearly that you hadn't killed anyone.

Japanese: ああ、あれ？ オレ、ウソなんてついてないぜ。
English: Hey now. I never told you any lies.

Japanese: だって、ジッサイにやったのは あのコロシヤだ。
English: The person who did the killing was that De Killer guy, right?

Japanese: ‥‥オレは控え室で、 ヒルネしていただけだもんな。
English: All I'm guilty of is taking a catnap in my room.

Japanese: ‥‥おまえは‥‥おまえは‥‥ イサオさんを殺したんだ！
English: You... You... You killed Mr. Corrida!

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。じゃ、明日の法廷で そう言ってみるかい？
English: Hahaha. I dare you to say that in court tomorrow.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥
English: Grr...

Japanese: いやいや。それはできない。 だってアンタ、オレの弁護士だ。
English: Aww, but too bad. You can't. You're my lawyer, after all. Aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、いっそオレの 弁護をやめちゃうか？
English: You could always drop my case and refuse to represent me. How does that sound?

Japanese: いやいや、それはもっとマズい。 それだけはできない。ゼッタイに。
English: Aww, but you can't, can you? That would be the one thing you absolutely can't do.

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵さまが‥‥！
English: M-Mystic Maya...!

Japanese: “ギリがたいコロシヤ”か。 ‥‥ホントだったみたいだな。
English: You wouldn't want to test De Killer. He's a man of his word, or so I hear.

Japanese: オレが有罪になったら、ダイジな お友だちが消えちまうんだって？
English: You could end up getting a certain friend of yours rubbed out if you lose.

Japanese: ‥‥キ‥‥キサマ‥‥
English: ...Y...You... scoundrel...

Japanese: というわけで、成歩堂弁護士サマ。 明日もよろしくおねがいしますよ。
English: So if I were you, Mr. Wright, esquire, I think I would give it my all tomorrow.

Japanese: おたがいのシアワセのために。
English: Remember, everyone likes a happy win-win resolution.

Japanese: かならず‥‥思い知らせてやる！
English: I... I'll get you for this!

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、そのフレーズ。
English: That's such a cliché phrase.

Japanese: たしか藤見野も そんなコト、言ってたっけ。
English: Juan said something just like that, if memory serves.

Japanese: ‥‥モチロン、 実現はしなかったけどね。
English: Of course... Well, we all know how well things turned out for him, don't we?

Japanese: じゃ。おやすみ、弁護士クン。
English: Good night, Mister Lawyer.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: （ぼくは‥‥どうすればいい！）
English: (Maya, what am I supposed to do!?)

Japanese: ‥‥これで‥‥
English: And now...

Japanese: キミもやっと たどりついたわけだ‥‥。
English: Now you've finally found it.

Japanese: この事件のスタートラインに。
English: The starting line of this case.
Edgeworth...

Japanese: ここはマズい。 ‥‥刑事課へ行こう。
English: I don't care for the horrid atmosphere here. Let's return to the precinct.

Japanese: 例の“記者会見”の ことですけど‥‥
English: Um, about the "press conference"...

Japanese: 藤見野がトノサマンを着て やろうとしていた、アレ？
English: You mean the one where Juan was gonna dress up as the Nickel Samurai?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。 霧緒さんが言っていました。
English: Yeah. I heard a little more about it from Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: どうやら藤見野は‥‥
English: It looked like somehow, Juan had in his hands a secret so powerful...

Japanese: 王都楼の芸能生命を断つような ヒミツを‥‥にぎっていたのです！
English: that it would destroy Matt's acting career had it been revealed!

Japanese: 教えてくれませんか。 その“ヒミツ”について。
English: Could you please fill me in on what this "secret" is? Please?

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew this was coming...)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？ も、もしかして‥‥
English: Mr. Nick? D-Don't tell me...

Japanese: （サイコ・ロック‥‥）
English: (A Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: “ヒミツ”と言われてもなあ。 心当たり、ありませんよ‥‥？
English: You said a "secret", right? But you don't have any idea what it is, do you dude...?

Japanese: 重要なことなんです。 ‥‥聞かせてください。
English: This is a very important matter! Please! You must tell me!

Japanese: あなたの“ヒミツ”を！
English: What is this "secret"!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですねー。
English: Hmm... Well...

Japanese: ちょっと待って。ボクの弁護士に 相談してみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna consult my lawyer, OK?

Japanese: は、はい。成歩堂です。
English: H-Hello? This is Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: あ。弁護士さん。
English: Oh, hey Mr. Lawyer dude.

Japanese: すみませんケド‥‥。 やっぱり、心当たりありませんよ。
English: Sorry, but you know dude, you totally don't have any idea.

Japanese: そもそもボク、藤見野のコトなんか 気にもかけてなかったしね。
English: And I really didn't care at all about what Juan was up to, OK?

Japanese: ま。そういうコトです。
English: So, it's like that.

Japanese: （解除するしかないか ‥‥サイコ・ロック）
English: (Looks like I have no choice but to remove those Psyche- Locks...)

Japanese: イサオさんと霧緒さんのコト‥‥ 知ってましたか？
English: Did you know about Mr. Corrida and Ms. Andrews' relationship...?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、週刊誌にも 書いてあったし、ね。
English: Well, it's all over the tabloids, dude.

Japanese: あ。でも、 詳しいことは知りませんよ。
English: Ah, but I don't know any of the details, if that's what you mean.

Japanese: 何度も言うけど、藤見野なんて キョーミありませんからね。
English: Look, how many times do I have to tell you? I don't care what Juan did with his life.

Japanese: 霧緒さんは‥‥目的があって イサオさんに近づいたんですよ。
English: Ms. Andrews... She had a purpose in mind when she started seeing Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: 彼女の先輩で、イサオさんの マネージャーだった‥‥、
English: Her mentor was Mr. Corrida's manager...

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さんの遺書を 手に入れるために。
English: and Ms. Andrews was going to get Ms. Celeste Inpax's suicide note from him.

Japanese: ユリエ‥‥
English: Celeste...

Japanese: 何か、心当たりは‥‥？
English: Does that jog any memories...?

Japanese: なんか、ハラへっちゃったな。 ピザでもたのみましょうか。
English: Dude, I suddenly just got totally hungry. You up for a pizza? My treat.

Japanese: あの、なるほどくん。 “ぴざ”というのは‥‥？
English: Um... Mr. Nick? What's a "pea-za"? Is it a kind of pea, like green peas?

Japanese: あとで食べさせてあげるよ。
English: Let's go eat one later, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥ピザ屋のヤツ、 断りやがった。
English: ...Argh, I got cut off by the pizza dude at the shop.

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょうね。
English: That's too bad...

Japanese: ‥‥さ。じゃあ気分を変えて、 他のハナシをしましょうか。
English: Well, how about we get our minds off of this topic and talk about something else, OK?

Japanese: （オートロさん‥‥）
English: (Mr. Engarde...)

Japanese: （由利恵さんの死に、何か 関係があるのか‥‥？）
English: (Are you connected to Ms. Inpax's suicide in some way...?)

Japanese: ‥‥やるねえ、アンタ。
English: Well done, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ここまで調べあげるとは‥‥ たいしたモンだぜ。
English: I bet it wasn't easy to gather as much information as you have.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥依頼したんですか？ 虎狼死家　左々右エ門に。
English: You really... So you were Shelly de Killer's client!?

Japanese: わざわざオレの手を ヨゴすこともないだろ？
English: You don't really think I would dirty my own hands in this, do you?

Japanese: な、なんということを‥‥！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: あの女‥‥霧緒も いいドキョウ、してるぜ。
English: And that woman... Adrian was quite brave herself.

Japanese: ‥‥このオレに 罪を着せようなんてさあ。
English: Trying to stick the crime on me -- I didn't think she had it in her.

Japanese: ついでだ。ヤツも死んでもらうか？ ‥‥なあ、弁護士クン。
English: But all I care about is that Juan is dead. Isn't that right, Mister Lawyer?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥ウ、ウソです！ ‥‥ヒドい‥‥。
English: Th-That's... You're lying!! What a terrible...!

Japanese: おじょうちゃん。 ‥‥コドモは寝る時間だ。
English: It's way past your bedtime... little girl.

Japanese: これ以上オトナのつまらん話に クビをつっこまないことだな。
English: Go on, and let us grown-ups talk about more... adult things.

Japanese: でも、どうして！ ビデオカメラなんて‥‥
English: But why!? Why did you hide the video camera and...

Japanese: 弱いヤツは、すぐ他人を信じる。 ‥‥あの、霧緒みたいに。
English: A weakling soon believes the words of others... just like that pathetic Adrian.

Japanese: （知ってたのか‥‥ 霧緒さんのこと）
English: (He knew about Ms. Andrews' secret...?)

Japanese: オレはちがう。だれも信じない。 ‥‥特に、殺し屋なんて人種はな。
English: But I'm no weakling. I don't believe anyone... least of all assassins.

Japanese: どういうことだ‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: “殺し屋”から“ゆすり屋”に 転職を考えるかもしれないだろ？
English: Oh, come now, Mr. Wright. Assassins aren't above blackmail.

Japanese: 殺人の依頼をネタにすりゃ、 いくらだってシボり取れる。
English: They turn their clients into cashcows by holding the sinful deed over their heads.

Japanese: 特にオレ、スーパースターだろ？ 脅迫するにはもってこいだ。
English: And a superstar like me, how much do you think I'm worth? Care to guess?

Japanese: だ、だから‥‥？
English: And... And that's why...?

Japanese: そこで、あのビデオが モノを言う、ってワケだ。
English: Yes, that's where the video comes in.

Japanese: アイツのカオも、犯行現場も バッチリ記録されているんだぜ。
English: It's got his face and the crime scene recorded on it, preserved for all time.

Japanese: こっちが逆に、ヤツを 脅迫することだってできる。
English: With that, I can keep him at bay, and even blackmail him if I want.

Japanese: そう‥‥ビデオは いわば“保険”ってヤツさ。
English: That's right. That video is my "insurance"... Isn't that what they call it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: なんて、きたないヒト‥‥
English: Why would you do something so wrong...?

Japanese: ‥‥オレ、オトナだから。 知らなかった？
English: Because I'm a grown-up, and I can. Good enough of an answer for you, little girl?

Japanese: いったい、なぜですか！ どうして、イサオさんを‥‥
English: Why!? Why would you kill Mr. Corrida...?

Japanese: こう見えてもオレって、 クリーンなイメージがウリだから。
English: Because he was about to sling so much dung onto my beautiful public image.

Japanese: メンドウなスキャンダルは、 ちょっとね。
English: Scandals are a little annoying, aren't they?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥あの記者会見か‥‥
English: This is all because of that press conference, isn't it...?

Japanese: イサオさんがバクロしようとした、 王都楼さんのヒミツ‥‥
English: If Mr. Corrida had been able to give it, then Mr. Engarde's secret would've...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、それが キッカケ、ってヤツかな。
English: Ah, well, that's what we call "taking advantage of the situation", you know?

Japanese: 前から気に入らなかったんだ。 目の前チョロチョロしやがってさ。
English: I had no interest in doing it, really, but bit by bit, it crept up on me.

Japanese: “こりゃ、ちょうどいいや” ‥‥って思ったワケ。
English: And then the situation just presented itself perfectly... "How beautiful," I thought.

Japanese: そんなことで、 イサオさんを‥‥
English: And that's... That's how Mr. Corrida ended up dead...

Japanese: オレはね。霧緒とちがって、 他人にたよったりはしないんだ。
English: Let me tell you something. I'm not like Adrian. I don't depend on anyone.

Japanese: 他人ってのはね、コキ使うモンだ。 ‥‥使い捨てだな。
English: People are simply things to be used. Used and thrown away.

Japanese: ちょっとアマいカオすりゃあいい。 ‥‥チョロいもんさ。
English: Put on a sweet, innocent face, and people will swallow anything you feed them.

Japanese: 霧緒もそう。殺し屋だってそう。 そして‥‥
English: Adrian fell for it. The assassin too. Oh, and how can I forget.

Japanese: モチロン、アンタもだ。 ‥‥弁護士クン！
English: Even you fell for it, Mister Lawyer!

Japanese: このオレ‥‥王都楼　真悟のために せいぜい、はたらいてくれよ。
English: Everyone, all working their butts off for me, Matt Engarde!

Japanese: コトバも出ないかい。 カワイソウにねえ‥‥。
English: Aww, did that leave you speechless? What a shame.

Japanese: なかなかいい トランシーバーですね。
English: That's a very nice transceiver, dude.

Japanese: ボク、けっこう 得意なんですよ。‥‥機械いじり。
English: I'm kind of an expert in gadgety things...

Japanese: （なんか、イヤな情報だな‥‥）
English: (Why does what he just said give me a sinking feeling?)

Japanese: 藤見野のヤツ‥‥
English: That Juan...

Japanese: ヒトの名前を使って、 記者会見なんて‥‥！
English: Posing as someone else and trying to hold a press conference...!

Japanese: しかも、手配したのは 霧緒なんですよね？
English: And the person who set it all up was Adrian, wasn't it?

Japanese: そうみたいですね。
English: That's what I heard.

Japanese: クソ‥‥アイツ、 どうしてこんなコトを‥‥！
English: Argh... I can't believe... Why would she do such a thing, dude!?

Japanese: ハッ！　ギターが シンボルマークとはね。
English: His signature item was a guitar. Hah!

Japanese: だいたい、なんで忍者が ギターなんですか？
English: I mean, dude, what kind of ninja carries a guitar!?

Japanese: い、いや、ぼくに 聞かれましても‥‥。
English: Um, I'm not someone who would know...

Japanese: まったく、コドモだましですね。 ボクに言わせれば。
English: Dude, it's such a kid thing to do. I mean, just my opinion and all.

Japanese: （‥‥たいして変わらない気が するけどな‥‥トノサマンも）
English: (Isn't your show a kid's show too?)

Japanese: キリオのヤツ‥‥ここまでして ボクに罪を着せようなんて‥‥
English: That Adrian... I never thought she'd go this far to frame me for the crime...

Japanese: いったい、ボクに なんのウラミがあるんだろう。
English: I mean, what does she have against me? Dude, I don't get it.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ぼくもぜひ、 知りたいですね。
English: That's something I would like to know too.

Japanese: 何か、心当たりが あるんじゃないですか？
English: Do you have any ideas at all? Even the slightest clue?

Japanese: な！　バ！　な！　バ‥‥ バカ言わないでくださいよ！
English: Huh!? What!? Me!? D-Don't be silly, Mr. Lawyer dude!

Japanese: 知るわけないじゃないですか。 ‥‥そんなの。
English: How am I supposed to know something like that?

Japanese: ‥‥生前のアイツ、ホントに 演技力がなかったんだけど‥‥
English: He was a bit of a talentless hack when he was alive...

Japanese: さすがにコイツは、 真にせまってますね。
English: But who knew he could play dead so well? Wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: あ、アタリマエですよ！ ホントに死んでるんですから。
English: W-Well, that's because he really IS dead!

Japanese: カワイソウにねえ‥‥。
English: And quite pitifully indeed, yes?

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼さん？
English: ...Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: たしかにボク、あなたに 弁護をたのみましたけどね。
English: Dude, I know I asked you to be my lawyer and all...

Japanese: 何もかも話すギムは ないと思うんですよ。
English: But I don't think I have to tell you anything and everything.

Japanese: それって‥‥ どういうことですか‥‥？
English: Um... What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 何もかも話すギムは ない、ってコトです。
English: It just means I don't have to tell you anything and everything, dude.

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: だ、ダメです、なるほどくん！
English: M-Mr. Nick! You can't!
Huh?

Japanese: キリオさん、そのことは だれにも知られたくないのですよ。
English: Ms. Andrews said she doesn't want anyone to know about that, remember?

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？　それ。
English: What's that?

Japanese: あ‥‥。いいです、やっぱり。
English: Oh, this? Um... Nothing. It's nothing, really.

Japanese: （‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ 見せるのが、ちょっとコワい）
English: I'm kind of scared to show him this card...)

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: What's wrong, dude?

Japanese: い、いえ。 あの‥‥これですけど。
English: Oh, um... So about this picture card...

Japanese: このカード‥‥ 見おぼえはありませんか？
English: Have you ever seen this before?

Japanese: さあ。見たことないですよ。
English: Nope, never saw it before in my life, dude.

Japanese: （ウソはついていない、か）
English: (I don't think he's lying... Or is he...?)

Japanese: （ホッとしたような‥‥ たいしてイミがないような‥‥）
English: (Then again, he looked like he gasped just now... Maybe I'm seeing things...?)

Japanese: これ、見てもらえますか？ ツマラナイものですけど。
English: Could you please take a look at this? I know it may not seem important to you...

Japanese: ツマラナイものを見るぐらいなら、 寝たほうがマシですね。
English: Well, if it's not important, then I'd rather be in bed.

Japanese: １２時間は寝ないとダメなんです。 おハダに悪いから。
English: If I don't get my 12 hours of beauty sleep, my skin's gonna wrinkle up like a prune, dude.

Japanese: そういえば、なるほどくんは ほっぺがカサカサですね。
English: You know, he's right Mr. Nick. Your skin's kind of drying up here and there.

Japanese: （この事件が解決したら ‥‥ゆっくり眠れるかな）
English: (I swear... After this case is over, I'm going to get lots and lots of sleep...)

Japanese: ‥‥見たことあるカオですね。 ファンクラブのヒトかな。
English: I've seen him somewhere before... Is he a member of my fan club?

Japanese: 荷星さんです。センパイでしょう？ ‥‥あなたの。
English: That's Mr. Powers. Isn't he supposed to be kind of like a mentor to you?

Japanese: ニボシ‥‥？　ああ、 体操のおニイさん、やってる。
English: Powers...? Oh yeah! He's the guy on that kid's exercise program.

Japanese: このあいだ、 サイン書いてあげましたよ。
English: I gave him an autograph just the other day.

Japanese: （まったく、センパイの立場が ないな‥‥）
English: (He doesn't see Mr. Powers as a mentor at all...)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、デビューも いっしょだったから‥‥
English: It's true we debuted at the same time...

Japanese: 何かと くらべられましたけど。
English: so that's why people were always comparing us.

Japanese: でも、ボクはゼンゼン 気にしてませんでしたね。
English: But I totally didn't care what people were saying because he wasn't even close, dude.

Japanese: だって‥‥いつも こっちが勝ってましたから。
English: I mean, I won every contest we ever had, hands down!

Japanese: ハンサムですよね。ホント。ボク。
English: Yeah... I'm such a handsome devil.

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ。
English: Uh... Um, yeah...

Japanese: 空手、柔道、抜刀道、ヤリに シュリケン、小刀護身道に居合道。
English: And I'm way good at sports! Karate, tennis, judo, soccer, football, hockey, fencing...

Japanese: そりゃあ、人気も出ますよね。
English: Dude, it's no wonder I'm so popular.

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ。 （悪気がないぶん、ハラ立つな）
English: Uh... Um, yeah... (You're also good at sitting in a jail cell, Mr. Popular.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、ボクに 罪を着せようなんて‥‥。
English: I don't get it... Why would she want to frame me...?

Japanese: いつも、メンドウを見て くれていたのに‥‥
English: I mean, I've never done anything to her, dude...

Japanese: （オートロさん、たよりなさそうに 見えるからな‥‥）
English: (Mr. Engarde sounds pretty sincere and he seems trustworthy...)

Japanese: （霧緒さんの“真意”‥‥ たしかに、知る必要がある！）
English: (So I have to find out what Ms. Andrews' real intentions are!)

Japanese: この人ですけど‥‥。
English: About this person...

Japanese: ‥‥こ、コイツは‥‥
執事の田中さん、ですよね。 お屋敷で、お会いしたんですよ。
English: He is your butler, Mr. Doe, right? We met him at your mansion.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そうですか。
English: Oh... Yes, that's right.

Japanese: なかなかよくデキた男ですよ。 ‥‥世話になってます。
English: He's a pretty cool dude who can do lots of things. He takes real good care of me.

Japanese: そのヒト、ファンクラブの 会員かな。
English: Um, dude, this person looks like a member of my fan club.

Japanese: ちょっと待って。ファンクラブの 会長さんに聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask the fan club president, OK?

Japanese: いえもう、けっこうです‥‥。
English: Um, "dude", you know what...? Forget I asked...

Japanese: ‥‥今さら、何を つきつけたってムダだ。
English: I don't care what you show...

Japanese: もう、じゅうぶん情報は くれてやっただろ？
English: I think you've gotten enough out of me, don't you?

Japanese: あとは、キミの仕事だ。 ‥‥弁護士クン。
English: Enough freebies for today. The rest is up to you, isn't that right... Mister Lawyer?

Japanese: ～王都楼のヒミツ～
English: -- Matt's Secret --

Japanese: 聞かせてもらいましょうか。 あなたの“ヒミツ”を‥‥。
English: Now, let's hear what this "secret" of yours is.

Japanese: イサオさんは、あの記者会見で 何を暴露しようとしていたか‥‥？
English: What if Mr. Corrida had been successful in his plan? What would he have disclosed...?

Japanese: だから‥‥知りませんよ。 藤見野のコトなんて。
English: I told you before, dude. I don't know! I don't know anything about Juan, OK?

Japanese: いいですか、成歩堂さん。 何度も言いますが‥‥、
English: Look, Mr. Wright. I can keep on saying it until I'm blue in the face, but...

Japanese: ボク、藤見野のコトなんて 意識してなかったんですよ。
English: I totally didn't pay Juan any attention the whole time that night!

Japanese: マッタク、ね。 ‥‥眼中になかったんですよ！
English: I mean, come on! I was in the middle of a nap!

Japanese: ‥‥それはウソだ。
English: Don't lie to me.
Huh?

Japanese: あなたはいつも、イサオさんを気に していた。授賞式の夜は特に‥‥
English: I know you paid close attention to Mr. Corrida, especially on that night!

Japanese: どうですか？　王都楼さん。
English: Well, Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: ちょっと待って。友だちに 相談してみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna consult my friend, OK?

Japanese: ハナで笑っとけ、だって。
English: He said I should snort while I give a good laugh.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um...

Japanese: じゃ、シツレイして。 ‥‥ハン！
English: OK, here I go. Aha ha ha ha *snort!*

Japanese: （こんなところで しくじるとは‥‥）
English: (I can't believe he can be so flippant at a time like this...)

Japanese: ‥‥オートロさん。 ハナで笑ってもムダですよ。
English: Um, Mr. Engarde... I don't think you need to snort while you laugh. Really.

Japanese: 事件当夜、イサオさんの控え室は カメラで盗撮されていたんですよ。
English: Someone used this camera to secretly film Mr. Corrida's room the night of the murder.
..."Secretly film"...? What?

Japanese: カメラの映像は、コイツで 外部に送られていました。
English: And then sent the images the camera took with this transmitter.

Japanese: へえ。‥‥いったい、どこに そんなカメラがあったんですか？
English: Wow... But dude, where was this camera you're talking about hidden?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。 ボク、つかれてるんですよ。
English: Mr. Lawyer dude, I'm really tired...

Japanese: わかっています。
English: I know...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。あなたのほうが、 もっとつかれてるみたいだ。
English: But dude, you look even more tired than me.

Japanese: もう、今日はイイカゲンにして、 休んだらどうですかね。
English: Why don't we call it quits for today and get some sleep? How does that sound?

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What made you say that all of a sudden?

Japanese: “ワケのわからないものを 見せるな”ってコト。
English: Well, you're here showing me stuff that doesn't make any sense.

Japanese: あ、なるほど。
English: Oh, oops.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、ホントに つかれているみたいだな）
English: (I guess I really must be tired...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、ここで 折れるワケには行かない！
English: But I'm not giving up just yet.

Japanese: 盗撮カメラは、コイツの目に しかけられていました。
English: The spy camera was hidden in this bear's eye!

Japanese: ファンからのプレゼントに まじって、ね！
English: A bear that was supposed to be a present from a fan!

Japanese: 事件当夜、イサオさんの控え室に あったものです。
English: This bear was in Mr. Corrida's room on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ファンからのプレゼントに まじって‥‥ね。
English: A bear that was supposed to be a present from a fan!

Japanese: ‥‥そいつはプレゼントじゃ ないんですか？
English: So? It's a present, dude.

Japanese: なかなかシャレた ぬいぐるみだと思いますよ。
English: It actually looks kinda classy, if you know what I mean.

Japanese: ええ。高級品らしいですよ。
English: Yes, I hear it's quite expensive.

Japanese: ‥‥なにしろ、このクマには こんな機能があるんですからね！
English: Which is why it is very odd that this gadget was found inside this bear!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。 ボク、つかれてるんですよ。
English: Mr. Lawyer dude, I'm really tired...

Japanese: わかっています。
English: I know...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。あなたのほうが、 もっとつかれてるみたいだ。
English: But dude, you look even more tired than me.

Japanese: もう、今日はイイカゲンにして、 休んだらどうですかね。
English: Why don't we call it quits for today and get some sleep? How does that sound?

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What made you say that all of a sudden?

Japanese: “ワケのわからないものを 見せるな”ってコト。
English: Well, you're here showing me stuff that doesn't make any sense.

Japanese: あ、なるほど。
English: Oh, oops.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、ホントに つかれているみたいだな）
English: (I guess I really must be tired...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、ここで 折れるワケには行かない！
English: But I'm not giving up just yet.

Japanese: 超小型のビデオカメラです。
English: This is a very small video camera.

Japanese: コイツの目に、 しかけられていました。
English: And it was hidden inside this bear's eye.

Japanese: ‥‥好奇心の強いクマですね‥‥
English: ...Maybe it's a really curious bear...?

Japanese: カメラの映像は、コイツで どこかに送信されていた。
English: Well, whatever this "bear" saw was sent somewhere using this transmitter.
Which means...

Japanese: 事件当夜、イサオさんの控え室は 盗撮されていたんですよ！
English: Someone, on the night of the murder, was secretly filming Mr. Corrida's room!

Japanese: ‥‥フン。
English: ...Hmph.

Japanese: あんなヤツでも、ファンは いたみたいですからね。
English: I guess Juan had a few of those kinds of fans too, huh dude?

Japanese: ‥‥これは、ファンからの プレゼントではないんですよ。
English: Actually, I wouldn't say this bear was a present from a "fan".

Japanese: ふうん‥‥そうなんですか。 じゃあいったい、だれからの？
English: Hmm... You sure, dude? Who else could it be from?

Japanese: このぬいぐるみを イサオさんにおくったのは‥‥
English: The person who gave this bear to Mr. Corrida was...

Japanese: どうですか？　王都楼さん。
English: Well, Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: ちょっと待って。おかあさんに 聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask my mom, OK?

Japanese: そんなヒトと友だちに なっちゃいけません、だって。
English: She says that I can't be friends with someone like you.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um...

Japanese: 帰ってください！ ケーサツを呼びますよ！
English: Go home! If you don't go away, I'm gonna call the police on you!

Japanese: （‥‥おかしいな。 カンタンだと思ったのに‥‥）
English: (...That's strange. I thought the answer was right there in front of me...)

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼さん。 このクマに、見おぼえは‥‥？
English: Mr. Engarde. Don't you know this bear from somewhere...?

Japanese: クマに知り合いは いないと思いますよ。
English: I don't think I've ever met Mr. Bear before, dude.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。コイツは、あなたを 知ってるみたいですよ？
English: Aww, but he says he knows you. How could you forget such a great friend?

Japanese: 何が言いたいんですか？
English: What else did the bear tell you?

Japanese: カメラをしかけたのは あなたです！　王都楼さん。
English: He says that the one who put the camera in his eye was you, Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 法廷のあなたを知らなければ、 あぶなくダマされるところですね。
English: If I didn't know how you work in court, I'd think I was in some serious trouble.

Japanese: ‥‥ハッタリ、でしょ？　どうせ。
English: Come on... This is all a joke, right dude? You're just pulling my leg.

Japanese: どうやら、認める気は ないみたいですね？
English: Looks like you're not ready to give up your secret yet.

Japanese: ‥‥その前に、証拠を 出す気はないんですか？
English: Well, do you have any proof you want to show me first?

Japanese: あなたがカメラをしかけた証拠。 それは‥‥
English: Here is proof that it was you who put the camera inside the bear:

Japanese: さあ、どうですか‥‥ オートロさん！
English: Well, come on, Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: ちょっと待って。おばあちゃんに 聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask my grandma, OK?

Japanese: 知ってました？　夜、口笛を ふいちゃ、ダメなんだそうです。
English: Did you know? It's bad to whistle at night, dude...

Japanese: ‥‥ヘビが出るから。
English: Because snakes will come out if you do.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um...

Japanese: ウチのおばあちゃん、 ものしりなんですよ。
English: My grandma, she knows all sorts of stuff.

Japanese: あなたも少し、 見ならったほうがいいですね！
English: I think you'd better learn from her example, OK?

Japanese: （早いハナシ、ちゃんと考えろ、 というコト‥‥なのか？）
English: (Is he telling me to think carefully before I speak...?)

Japanese: （とにかく、 もう一度やりなおしてみよう！）
English: (Argh! So close and yet so far!)

Japanese: ここにクレジットカードの 伝票があります。
English: I have here, one credit card receipt, Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 王都楼さん‥‥あなたが ぬいぐるみを買ったときのものだ！
English: It's from when YOU bought that stuffed bear!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなの、３８万円っていう 金額を証明するだけでしょう？
English: Dude, all you can tell from this is that I spent $3,800.

Japanese: ボク、あのデパートでよく 買い物しますからね。
English: I go to that department store all the time, OK?

Japanese: この３８万円なら‥‥アレかも しれないな。歯ブラシ。
English: This $3,800... This could be the toothbrush I bought that one time.

Japanese: さ、さんじゅうはちまんえんの 歯ブラシですか？
English: A-A THIRTY-EIGHT HUNDRED DOLLAR TOOTHBRUSH!?

Japanese: ‥‥象牙で象毛だったから。
English: ...It's ivory... And it's got elephant hair for bristles...

Japanese: （それって高級なのか‥‥？）
English: (Eww... Elephant hair? Is that what rich people use nowadays...?)

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway!

Japanese: おもちゃ売り場の店員が、 あなたをおぼえていたんですよ。
English: The store clerk clearly remembers you and your purchase.

Japanese: サインまでしてあげた そうじゃないですか！
English: After all, you even gave him an autograph, did you not!?

Japanese: なんだ。‥‥それを 先に言ってよ。
English: Dude... You should've said that earlier.

Japanese: あのさ。 １つ、聞いていいかな？
English: Um, so can I ask you one thing?

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Yes?

Japanese: あなた、ボクの弁護士ですよね。
English: You're my lawyer, right dude?

Japanese: なんで、そんなことを 調べなきゃならないのかな。
English: So, if you are, then why are you looking into stuff like that?

Japanese: ‥‥真実を知っていなければ、 弁護なんてできませんからね。
English: Because if I don't know the truth, I can't help you.

Japanese: バカバカしいね。 ‥‥ね。もういいかな、ボク。
English: Sounds more like stupid lawyer talk to me... Hey, let's stop talking about this, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥まだです。
English: No, not yet.

Japanese: こんなものをしかけた 理由を聞いていませんからね。
English: I haven't asked why you set the camera up yet.

Japanese: それこそ、あなたの“ヒミツ”だ。 決して、だれにも言えない‥‥
English: And what your "secret" is. Of course, it would be strictly confidential...

Japanese: だとしたら‥‥ どうなんです？
English: So...? What are you going to do now?

Japanese: 聞き出します。 ‥‥なんとしても。
English: I'm going to find out what I want to know... because I must.

Japanese: あなたがイサオさんの部屋を 盗撮した、その理由は‥‥
English: The reason you hid this camera in Mr. Corrida's room and filmed it in secret is...

Japanese: オートロさん‥‥？
English: Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: そのクマを持って帰って、 そいつとおしゃべりしたらどう？
English: Dude, how about you take that bear home with you and talk to it?

Japanese: “しゃべらない”っていう点では 同じだから。ボクと。
English: I'm sure Mr. Bear will talk with you about as much as I will, which is not at all.

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: （‥‥これだけはハッキリ させておかなければ‥‥）
English: (I have to somehow get it through his head I'm not giving up.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか？ あきらめてくれたかな。
English: So, is it time to throw in the towel yet, dude?

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥
English: Sorry, but no.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥
English: Adrian Andrews?

Japanese: イサオさんと霧緒さんが、 ヒミツで会っている‥‥
English: There is a rumor going around...

Japanese: そんなウワサがあります。
English: That Ms. Andrews and Mr. Corrida were having secret meetings.

Japanese: イサオさんを意識していた あなたは‥‥、
English: You, who was keeping tabs on Mr. Corrida...

Japanese: そのスキャンダルを 暴こうとしたんじゃないですか？
English: You were going to reveal this as fact, and turn it into a scandal. Isn't that right!?

Japanese: ‥‥バカバカしい。
English: ...Dude, you can be such a moron.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そういうスキャンダルはね、 弁護士さん。
English: Oh man, Mr. Lawyer dude, that kind of scandal...

Japanese: ボクたちにとっては オイシイ話なんですよ。
English: That's the good stuff! That's what we in the industry call "juicy"!

Japanese: ‥‥オイシイ‥‥？
English: ...The "good stuff"...? "Juicy"...?

Japanese: カネもかけずに世間が さわいでくれるんだからね。
English: Look, we can get publicity without spending a penny with that kind of stuff.

Japanese: 逆に言えば、さわがれなくなったら オシマイ、ってコト。
English: I mean, if people stopped paying attention to us, then it'd be the end, dude.

Japanese: ザンネンです。
English: Too bad, that wasn't your intention.

Japanese: ‥‥何がですか？
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: ‥‥できれば、こういうシンプルな 理由であってほしかった。
English: I wish your reason for spying was something so innocent. But it wasn't.

Japanese: 盗撮の目的が、霧緒さんじゃ なかったとすれば‥‥
English: You didn't spy on Mr. Corrida because of Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: 残る可能性は、 １つしかありません。
English: Then there's only one reason I can think of for you to do such a thing.

Japanese: あなたがイサオさんの控え室に カメラを仕掛けた理由は‥‥
English: The real reason you set up that camera in Mr. Corrida's room was...

Japanese: ‥‥もしボクが カメラをしかけるなら‥‥
English: ...If I really wanted to set up a spy camera...

Japanese: 藤見野の控え室なんかじゃない。 あなたの事務所にしますね。
English: I wouldn't have set it up in Juan's room. It would be in your office, dude.

Japanese: え。ぼくの事務所‥‥？
English: Huh? My office...?

Japanese: いつも、こんなトンチンカンなコト を言ってるんでしょう？
English: Well, dude, you're always saying really absurd things, right?

Japanese: 見ていて笑えますからね！
English: It'd be like getting the comedy channel for free!

Japanese: （くそ！　最後のさいごで シッパイしたか‥‥）
English: (Argh! I'm so close! I can feel it!)

Japanese: と、とにかく‥‥！
English: I-If you're done laughing!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、そのカード。
English: Wh-What is this card...?

Japanese: （コイツのこと、 知らないのか‥‥）
English: (Maybe he doesn't know about this card...)

Japanese: これは、ある男の 名刺‥‥みたいなものです。
English: This is a certain man's "calling card"...

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門。 ‥‥もちろん、ご存じですね。
English: The man's name is Shelly de Killer... and I'm sure you know of him, don't you?

Japanese: コロシヤ‥‥サザエモン‥‥
English: Shelly... de Killer...

Japanese: バ、バ‥‥バカな！
English: Th-Th-That's ridiculous!

Japanese: な、な、な、な、なんでボクが、 そんなヤツのことを‥‥
English: Wh-Why would I know some shady scumbag like him...?

Japanese: 彼を知らないのならば‥‥ なぜそんなに動揺してるんですか？
English: If you really don't know him, then why are you acting so jumpy all of a sudden?

Japanese: ‥‥うっ！
English: Umm!

Japanese: （認めたくなかった ‥‥これだけは！）
English: (This is it! I'm finally starting to get to the truth!)

Japanese: （‥‥でも‥‥ もう、まちがいない‥‥）
English: (I can't afford to make any more mistakes now...)

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟さん。
English: Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたがなぜ、虎狼死家を 知っているのか‥‥？
English: I know why you know Mr. de Killer. It's because...

Japanese: それは、あなたが正義のヒーロー トノサマンだからです！
English: It's because you're the great hero of justice, the Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: えええっ！
English: Duuuuude!?

Japanese: あなたは虎狼死家　左々右エ門の インボーを暴くため‥‥
English: You spied on that room to do one thing and one thing only!

Japanese: あの部屋を見張っていたのです！
English: To expose Shelly de Killer's fiendish plot!

Japanese: あなたがそれでいいのなら、 ボクはかまわないけど。
English: ...Okay... If that's what you think, then whatever, dude.

Japanese: （こりゃマズいか）
English: (I get the feeling that wasn't quite it...)

Japanese: す、すみません。 つい、ウッカリ‥‥。
English: S-Sorry. Slip of the tongue...

Japanese: それは、あなたが スターだからです！
English: It's because you're a star!

Japanese: えええっ！ ど、どういうイミですか？
English: Duuuuude!? What in the world are you talking about!?

Japanese: スターにリクツはいらない！ 必要なのは‥‥オーラだけです！
English: Well, you're a star. And stars never need a logical, or sane reason for anything!

Japanese: このカメラを セットしたということは‥‥
English: Since you're the one who set up that camera...

Japanese: あなたは知っていたんです。 ‥‥あの部屋で、何が起こるかを。
English: That means you knew... You knew exactly what was going to happen in that room!

Japanese: そして‥‥なぜ、あなたが それを知っていたか‥‥？
English: So... How? How would you know something like that...?

Japanese: 答えは１つしかない。 ‥‥あなたが依頼したからだ。
English: It's because you're his client. That's why.

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門に‥‥ 藤見野　イサオの暗殺を！
English: You hired Shelly de Killer to assassinate Mr. Juan Corrida!

Japanese: この事件の真犯人は‥‥
English: The real mastermind behind this whole murder is...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟！ あなただったんだ！
English: You, Matt Engarde!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥
English: ...*sigh*

Japanese: ‥‥せっかく、 イイ子にしていてあげたのに。
English: And here I was, trying to be a good boy for you, dude.

Japanese: 知らないほうが、あなたも キモチよく仕事ができる。
English: I thought if you didn't know, you'd be able to do your job without feeling bad.

Japanese: ‥‥そう思ったのになあ‥‥
English: Well, that's what I thought, anyway...

Japanese: お‥‥王都楼さん‥‥ やっぱり、あなたが‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Engarde... You really did hire...!?

Japanese: ちょっと待って。ボクと 相談してみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna consult myself, OK?

Japanese: （“ボク”と‥‥相談する？）
English: (Consult... "myself"...??)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろいいだろう、 だってさ。
English: ...Well, I guess it's probably about time anyway.

Japanese: な、何が‥‥
English: About time for what?

Japanese: 弁護士さんにアイサツしても。
English: I think it's time for you to meet him now, Mr. Lawyer dude.

Japanese: 初めまして‥‥弁護士クン。 王都楼　真悟だ。
English: How do you do... Mister Lawyer? I'm Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥あら。あなたでしたか。
English: ...Oh, it's you.

Japanese: 夜もおそいのに、すみません。
English: I'm sorry to be visiting at such a late hour.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、どうしても 話を聞いておきたかったんです。
English: But there are a few questions I absolutely have to ask you tonight.

Japanese: ‥‥私に？
English: Me?

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は、たしか 王都楼　真悟じゃなかったかしら？
English: I thought your client was Matt.

Japanese: （この人は、何か 知っているはずだ‥‥）
English: (I'm sure Ms. Andrews knows something...)

Japanese: （藤見野　イサオがにぎっていた オートロさんの“ヒミツ”‥‥）
English: (She can't be clueless about this "secret" Mr. Corrida had on Mr. Engarde...)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitor's the visitor's room.

Japanese: 夜もふけて、眠いのだろう。 立ったまま寝ている。
English: He's sleeping standing up. The night's pretty long, and he probably got tired.

Japanese: ‥‥いいのか？
English: Hmm... I hope he doesn't fall over...

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: オートロさんのこと ですけど‥‥
English: I'd like to ask you about Matt Engarde, if you don't mind...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。‥‥まだ、 わからないんですか？
English: Mr. Wright... You still don't know, do you?

Japanese: 彼の正体が‥‥。
English: The real him, I mean...

Japanese: どうやらあなたは、オートロさんに 強い悪意があるようですね。
English: You seem to bear a lot of resentment towards Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: それならば、なぜ‥‥オートロさん のマネージャーになったんですか？
English: If that's the case, then why did you become his manager?

Japanese: それに、わざわざ彼のライバルの イサオさんに近づいたりして‥‥
English: And why would you become intimate with his rival?

Japanese: それは‥‥事件とは関係ないわ。 おそらく、ね。
English: That... has nothing to do with this case. Nothing.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さんの ことですけど‥‥
English: About Ms. Celeste Inpax...

Japanese: せっかく忘れかけていたのに‥‥
English: I had finally put her death behind me...

Japanese: あなたがたのおかげで、 また思い出してしまいました。
English: And now, thanks to you, it's all come back to the surface.

Japanese: それは‥‥すみません。
English: I... I'm sorry.

Japanese: たしかに、彼女が自殺したのは ショックでした。
English: Yes, I was shocked by her suicide.

Japanese: 藤見野が遺書をかくしたという ウワサを聞いて‥‥、
English: And it's true that when I heard the rumor that Juan was the one

Japanese: 彼に近づいたというのも、 ジジツです。
English: who had hidden her suicide note, I began to draw close to him.

Japanese: 遺書を取り返して‥‥ 燃やしてしまいたかった。
English: I wanted to get her suicide note back... and to burn it.

Japanese: “燃やす”‥‥ 遺書を、ですか。
English: You wanted to "burn it"? But why?

Japanese: ‥‥世間の目に ふれさせたくなかったんです。
English: I didn't want it to spread like just another piece of gossip.

Japanese: でも‥‥そのために、 藤見野を殺害するなんて。
English: But... I never held any murderous intent towards Juan.

Japanese: 私は、そんなバカなことは しません。
English: I would never do something so stupid.

Japanese: （遺書、か‥‥ 何が書いてあったんだろう？）
English: (The suicide note, huh...? I wonder what it said?)

Japanese: どうして‥‥オートロさんに 罪を着せようとしたんですか？
English: Why did you try to frame Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: カンタンな話。 ‥‥彼が犯人だからよ。
English: That's simple. Because he's the killer, that's why.

Japanese: 警察に知らせるのが 市民のギムってものでしょ？
English: Isn't it the duty of every good citizen to inform the police?

Japanese: で、でも‥‥他に、 やりかたがあったはずです。
English: B-But... there had to be another way.

Japanese: 警察は優秀だ。こうなることは わかっていたでしょう？
English: The police are excellent at doing their job, so they'd figure it out, right?

Japanese: その優秀な警察も、由利恵さんの 自殺の真相はわからなかった。
English: Yes, they're so good that they couldn't figure out the real truth behind Celeste's death.

Japanese: キリオさん‥‥。
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: とにかく。あなたは、目的もなく あんなことをする人じゃない。
English: Well, um, I know you're not the type of person to do something without a reason,

Japanese: ‥‥理由を教えてください。
English: so please, tell me why you did what you did...

Japanese: ‥‥フクシュウ‥‥
English: ...Revenge.

Japanese: え‥‥。今、 なんとおっしゃいましたか？
English: Huh...? Did you say something just now...?

Japanese: （ここでサイコ・ロックか‥‥）
English: (A Psyche-Lock, huh...)

Japanese: ‥‥わかってるでしょう？
English: Don't you understand yet?

Japanese: あなたは、私の弁護士じゃない。 それどころか‥‥敵なのよ。
English: You're not my lawyer. To be honest, you're more like my enemy.

Japanese: そんな‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: （今、たしかに‥‥ 霧緒さん、こう言ったぞ）
English: (I'm sure I just heard Ms. Andrews say,)
("Revenge".)

Japanese: どうして、オートロさんに 罪を着せようと‥‥？
English: Why did you frame Mr. Engarde...?

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしてたのね、きっと。
English: Hmm, I wonder why...? I don't really know myself.

Japanese: 気がついたら、 ナイフを刺していたの。
English: When I realized what I was doing, I had already stabbed the knife into the body.

Japanese: （さっき、霧緒さんは こうつぶやいた‥‥）
English: (I'm almost sure that just now...)
(Ms. Andrews muttered, "Revenge" to herself.)

Japanese: この中に、トノサマンの 着ぐるみが入ってたんですね？
English: So there was a Nickel Samurai costume inside of this case, correct?

Japanese: ええ、そうよ。
English: Yes, there was.

Japanese: スタジオから 持ち出したのは、私。
English: I even personally carried it here from the studio.

Japanese: わざわざそんなものを着て、 コドモみたい、って思ったけど‥‥
English: I always thought it was such a childish thing to dress up and wear a costume like that,

Japanese: まさか、この私が 使うことになるなんて、ね。
English: but I never thought that I would wind up using it myself that night.

Japanese: 記者会見を手配したのは 私です。
English: I was the one who organized the whole press conference.

Japanese: 藤見野は、王都楼に致命的な ダメージを与えようとしていた。
English: Juan wanted to deal the most damage possible to Matt's career.

Japanese: だから、殺されてしまった。 ‥‥わかりやすい話でしょ？
English: And that's why he was killed. It's quite simply a story of cause and effect, yes?

Japanese: グラスにジュースを そそいだとき‥‥
English: When I was pouring that glass of juice...

Japanese: 私、本当に気がついていなかった。 ‥‥藤見野が死んでいた、なんて。
English: I honestly had no idea that Juan was dead...

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ヒドい女よね。一度は 介抱しようと思ったクセに。
English: I really am a terrible woman, aren't I? One moment, I'm thinking of looking after him,

Japanese: 次の瞬間には、ムネにナイフを 刺しているんだから‥‥。
English: and the next, I'm stabbing him in the chest with a knife.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。私のアタマの中には、 ひとつのことしかなかったわ。
English: You know... There was only one thought running through my head at that time.

Japanese: “これで、王都楼の本性を 暴くことができる”って‥‥
English: "With this, I can finally expose Matt's true nature to the world."

Japanese: （オートロさんの “本性”か‥‥）
English: (His "true nature"...?)

Japanese: （なぜ霧緒さんは、それを 教えてくれないんだろう？）
English: (I wonder why she's saying that, but not telling me what his true nature is?)

Japanese: （もしぼくなら、よろこんで ブチまけるような気がするけど）
English: (If it were me, I think I'd be happy to finally get that kind of thing off my chest.)

Japanese: これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: About this photo...

Japanese: ‥‥ナイフを刺したのは私よ。 ギターケースを開けたのも、ね。
English: The person who stabbed the body and opened the guitar case was me.

Japanese: トノサマンの衣装を 着るため、ですか‥‥。
English: And that was so you could get the Nickel Samurai's costume out so you could wear it...?

Japanese: うまくやったと 思っていたんだけど。
English: I thought I had executed that part quite well.

Japanese: さすが、弁護士さんね。
English: But you showed me differently. You are a formidable lawyer, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: たとえ、 どんな理由があったにせよ‥‥
English: I don't care what the reason was...

Japanese: ユルせないわ。 ‥‥遺書をかくすなんて。
English: It's unforgivable that he hid Celeste's suicide note.

Japanese: でも、彼女が遺書を書いたという 証拠は、ないんですよ‥‥？
English: But you don't have any proof that she left a suicide note behind, right?

Japanese: 先輩は‥‥由利恵さんは、何ごと にもケジメをつけるヒトだった。
English: Celeste... She always believed there was a right way and a wrong way to do anything.

Japanese: 遺書はあったはずです。 私は‥‥そう信じているわ。
English: She would have left a note. I... I wholly believe that.

Japanese: ‥‥やめてッ！
English: S-Stop it!

Japanese: あのときの絶望的な気持ち‥‥ 思い出したくないの！
English: I don't want to remember the hopelessness and the despair I felt back then!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 早く、しまいましょう！
English: Hurry up and put that away, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: す‥‥すみません。
English: I-I'm so sorry!

Japanese: そのウワサ‥‥藤見野が 自分で流したみたいです。
English: It appears to me that rumor was started by Juan himself.

Japanese: 自分で‥‥？
English: Mr. Corrida...? But...

Japanese: 私、王都楼のマネージャー でしたから。
English: I'm Matt's manager, remember.

Japanese: 私とつきあうことで、王都楼に 恥をかかせるつもりだったみたい。
English: I think he wanted to embarrass Matt through his relationship with me.

Japanese: で、でも！　イサオさんは 霧緒さんのコトを‥‥！
English: But... But didn't Mr. Corrida like you...!?

Japanese: 愛情なんてなかったわ。
English: There weren't any feelings of love between us.

Japanese: 私は、由利恵さんの遺書を 手に入れるため‥‥
English: For me, it was about getting Celeste's suicide note...

Japanese: 彼は、王都楼のプライドを キズつけるため‥‥
English: For him, it was about wounding Matt's pride.

Japanese: それだけの関係だったの。
English: That's all it was.

Japanese: ‥‥そんな‥‥
English: That's... That's...

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、 ショックみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Poor Pearls is in shock. She sees just how cruel the world can really be.)

Japanese: 藤見野の死体のそばに 置かれていたんです。
English: It was near Juan's dead body.

Japanese: 私‥‥グラスを置いたときに 気がついたの。
English: I... I noticed it when I went to fill the glass.

Japanese: 死んでることに気がついて ‥‥私、完全にパニックだった。
English: And then when I realized that Juan was dead... I completely panicked.

Japanese: 自分でも気がつかないうちに ポケットにしまっていたの。
English: That's when I must have unconsciously picked this card up and put it into my pocket.

Japanese: 理由なんて‥‥わからないわ。
English: As for why...? I simply don't know.

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, I'd like to ask you about this...

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずです。 ムダなコトはキライだ、と。
English: I told you. I hate trifling matters.

Japanese: 私に関係ないものを 見せられても、困ります。
English: It's a waste of time to show me things that are of no relevance to me.

Japanese: （こんなに強く“キョゼツ” を感じるの、初めてだな‥‥）
English: (Wow... This is the first time I've been shut down this badly...)

Japanese: 荷星　三郎さん。 ‥‥私たち英都プロの俳優です。
English: That's Mr. Will Powers. He's an actor contracted with Global Studios.

Japanese: いい人です、とても。
English: He's a very nice man. A man with a good heart.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、それだけですけど。
English: ...But that's all he is and ever will be.

Japanese: （ひゃあ‥‥ヒドいこと言うな）
English: (Urk... What a horrible thing to say...)

Japanese: ‥‥私たちの世界は、いい人には 向いていないのです。
English: ...Society doesn't care about genuinely nice people, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: プライドの高いヒト‥‥でした。
English: He is a very prideful man... Or rather, was.

Japanese: 何かといえば王都楼と 張り合って‥‥
English: He absolutely had to compete with Matt in everything, no matter what it was.

Japanese: ホント‥‥バカみたい。
English: He really was such an idiot.

Japanese: キリオさん‥‥？
English: Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: スターなんて‥‥ みんなそうなのかもしれない。
English: Well, I guess maybe all stars are like that.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトの気持ちなんか‥‥ なんとも思っていないのよ。
English: Never giving any thought to other people's feelings.

Japanese: キリオさん‥‥？
English: Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: こうなってしまった以上‥‥
English: That things have come to this...

Japanese: もう、彼のことは 話したくありません。
English: I have nothing left to say about that man.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトコトも。
English: Not one word.

Japanese: 自分のことを話すのは 大キライです。
English: I hate talking about myself.

Japanese: ‥‥ムダなこと、ですから。
English: It's a trifling matter, that's why.

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: 由利恵さんは‥‥私の先輩でした。
English: Celeste... was my mentor.

Japanese: 強いひとだった。 そうカンタンに、自殺なんて‥‥
English: She was a strong woman... She wouldn't kill herself over any old trifling matter...

Japanese: も、もしかして‥‥
English: S-So...

Japanese: 由利恵さんの“自殺”‥‥ 疑っているのですか？
English: You have some ideas of why she killed herself...?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。どうかしら‥‥。
English: Yes, I suppose...

Japanese: ～罪を着せた理由～
English: -- Why Frame Him? --

Japanese: 聞かせてもらいましょう‥‥ 王都楼さんに罪を着せた理由を。
English: Can you please tell me why you framed Mr. Engarde for the murder?

Japanese: 何度も言ったでしょう。 王都楼が犯人だと思ったから。
English: I've already told you countless times. It's because I thought Matt was the killer.

Japanese: （ちがう！　霧緒さんは 王都楼さんに悪意を持っている）
English: (No, that's not it! I know you have a personal reason to dislike Mr. Engarde.)

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さん。さっき、ポツリと こう言いましたね？
English: Ms. Andrews. You may think I didn't hear it, but I know you said something earlier.

Japanese: “フクシュウ”‥‥と。
English: You said, "Revenge."

Japanese: ‥‥私が王都楼に復讐を したと言うの‥‥？
English: So you are saying I was taking my revenge out on Matt, and that's why?

Japanese: ば、バカバカしい。 私には‥‥そんな理由がないわ。
English: Wh-What an absurd idea. I... I don't have anything I want to take revenge for.

Japanese: （‥‥いつも、他人の言うとおりに しか行動できない霧緒さん‥‥）
English: (Ms. Andrews... A woman who lives by being dependant on another person...)

Japanese: （そんな彼女に復讐を 決意させた事件がある‥‥）
English: (There is something or someone in her past that would make her take revenge...)

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでして、 王都楼を助けたいんですか‥‥？
English: Are you trying to help or hurt Matt with what you're doing...?

Japanese: “そこまで”‥‥？
English: What? What am I doing?

Japanese: デタラメな証拠を つきつけてまで‥‥よ。
English: This random, haphazard throwing out of evidence you're so fond of doing.

Japanese: そ、そんなこと 考えてませんよ！
English: Th-That's not what I was trying to do at all!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、そんなもの、 引っこめてくださいな。
English: Then refrain from doing it anymore.

Japanese: （ハデにまちがったようだ‥‥）
English: (I guess I screwed up big again...)

Japanese: ゆ‥‥、由利恵さん‥‥
English: C-Celeste...

Japanese: あなたが“復讐”を考えるほど 強い感情を持つとしたら‥‥
English: There is only one catalyst that could cause such strong feelings, and even revenge.

Japanese: ‥‥その原因は、彼女の自殺しか 考えられません。
English: And that is Ms. Inpax's suicide.

Japanese: な、何を言ってるの‥‥？
English: Wh-What are you trying to say?

Japanese: 由利恵さんは、藤見野　イサオの マネージャーだったのよ。
English: Celeste was Juan's manager.

Japanese: それに！　遺書をかくしたと 言われてるのも、藤見野　イサオ。
English: On top of that, the one who hid her suicide note was also Juan.

Japanese: ‥‥どこに王都楼　真悟の 名前が出てくるって言うの！
English: What does all this have to do with Matt?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: You're right. You haven't mentioned him... Yet.

Japanese: あなたが王都楼さんを ニクんでいるということは‥‥
English: But for you to hate Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: 由利恵さんの自殺と王都楼は、 つながっていなければならない。
English: It would mean that he must have had some relation to Ms. Inpax and her suicide.

Japanese: その‥‥つながりを証明できるの？ 由利恵さんと‥‥王都楼の！
English: Can you explain to me this "relation" between Celeste and Matt!?

Japanese: ‥‥それが？
English: That's it?
Yes...

Japanese: 今の証拠が、何かのつながりを 証明してると言うのかしら？
English: And what sort of relation is this supposed to explain?

Japanese: だ、だから、由利恵さんと 王都楼がですね‥‥
English: W-Well, Ms. Inpax and Mr. Engarde's...

Japanese: その前に、成歩堂さん。
English: Before you start, Mr. Wright,

Japanese: あなたの脳ミソの配線が、キチンと つながっている証拠を見せて。
English: perhaps I should first request to see proof that your brain is wired correctly.

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ。 ここが最大のポイントなんだ）
English: (Argh. I can't afford to mess up something as big as this!)

Japanese: （由利恵さんと王都楼をつなぐ、 まぎれもない証拠‥‥）
English: (I need evidence that clearly shows a connection between Ms. Inpax and Mr. Engarde.)

Japanese: （そいつが、すべてを 解決してくれる‥‥！）
English: (If I can find that, I can finally get the real story!)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、うかがって いいかしら？　成歩堂さん。
English: I wonder if you'd like to try again, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: これ‥‥由利恵さんの 写真ですよね。
English: This... This is a photo of Ms. Inpax, correct?

Japanese: 亡くなったときより、 若く見えますけど。
English: She looks younger than when she passed away though.

Japanese: “愛をこめて。ユリエ” ‥‥由利恵さんの字ですよね？
English: "With love... Celeste". This is Ms. Inpax's handwriting, isn't it?

Japanese: どこで手に入れたのか‥‥ 聞く必要もないみたいね。
English: Where did you find this...? No, that's alright. It was a rhetorical question...

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah, it is.

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼さんの私室で 見つけました。
English: I found this at Mr. Engarde's mansion.

Japanese: ‥‥とうとう‥‥
English: ...And after all this time...

Japanese: たどりついたってわけね。 ‥‥私のヒミツに‥‥
English: My last remaining secret has been revealed...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: March 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: こんばんは、成歩堂さん。
English: Good evening, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたんですか？ まっ青よ‥‥顔色が‥‥
English: What's wrong? You look ill...

Japanese: 霧緒さん。
English: Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: あなたのサイコ・ロックを はずしに来ました。
English: I have come to remove your Psyche-Lock.

Japanese: サイコ・ロック‥‥？
English: Psyche-Lock...?

Japanese: 何がなんでも 聞かせてもらいます。
English: I want to know, and you will tell me...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのヒミツを。
English: ...your secret.

Japanese: いいわ。 ‥‥できるものなら、ね。
English: Fine, go ahead. Try to break me, if you can.

Japanese: 彼女‥‥結婚するはずだったわ。 藤見野　イサオと。
English: Celeste... She was supposed to get married to Juan.

Japanese: たしか‥‥ダメになった、 って聞きました。
English: Yes... But I heard that it didn't work out.

Japanese: イサオさんが、一方的に 婚約を解消したんですよね‥‥
English: Because Mr. Corrida didn't want to get married to her anymore, right...?

Japanese: そうよ。 王都楼　真悟のせいでね。
English: Yes... Because of Matt.

Japanese: お‥‥オートロさんのせい‥‥？
English: B-Because of Mr. Engarde? What do you mean?

Japanese: （なんとなく、見えてきたな）
English: (I think I can see where this is going.)

Japanese: 由利恵さん‥‥、ムカシは、 王都楼のマネージャーだったの。
English: Celeste... She was Matt's manager a long time ago.

Japanese: あのころの彼女は、 とても幸せそうだった‥‥
English: She was the happiest woman in the world at that time.

Japanese: 当時、アルバイトをしていた私は、 よく２人のすがたを見ていたわ。
English: I was working part-time back then, and I often saw the two of them together.

Japanese: ‥‥あの写真立て‥‥
English: So that's why...

Japanese: “愛をこめて”って 書いてありましたね。
English: "With love... Celeste" is written on the frame of that picture.

Japanese: おふたりは‥‥ おつきあいしていたのですね？
English: They were a couple, weren't they?

Japanese: そんなリッパなものじゃ なかったわ。
English: It wasn't anything as splendid as that.

Japanese: 由利恵さんは王都楼に遊ばれて‥‥ ‥‥そして、捨てられたわ。
English: Celeste was being used; toyed with until she was... thrown away.

Japanese: そんな‥‥。
English: That's so horrible...

Japanese: 王都楼は、サワヤカな イメージで売っていたから、
English: Matt's entire image is built around how nice and wonderful of a man he is.

Japanese: 当然、スキャンダルは もみ消されたわ‥‥強引に。
English: A scandal would have destroyed that.

Japanese: 由利恵さんは‥‥それで 光映プロダクションに移ったの。
English: Which is why Celeste, in her kindness, moved over to Worldwide Studios.

Japanese: そして、今度は 藤見野　イサオと出会った。
English: And that is where she met Juan.

Japanese: さいわい、藤見野は王都楼より マシに見えたわ。
English: She seemed really happy with him; even happier than when she was with Matt.

Japanese: ふたりは、婚約するところまで 行ったのだから。
English: Celeste and Juan were such a good match that they were even planning to get married.

Japanese: でも‥‥ケッキョク、 ダメになったわ。
English: And then it was suddenly called off.

Japanese: 婚約が解消された晩 ‥‥由利恵さんは亡くなった。
English: On the night Juan called their marriage off, Celeste... She killed herself.

Japanese: これは‥‥ 私と由利恵さんの復讐なの。
English: And that's why I framed Matt. It was revenge for Celeste and for myself.

Japanese: 藤見野が婚約を解消した理由 ‥‥わかるでしょう？
English: I'm sure even you can guess why Juan called the wedding off, right?

Japanese: 王都楼が、藤見野にバラしたの。 自分と‥‥由利恵さんの関係を。
English: Matt "confessed" to Juan... about his relationship with Celeste.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: I see... So that's what happened.

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも！
English: But...

Japanese: どうしてそんなことで 婚約を解消してしまうんですか！
English: Then why did Mr. Corrida have to call off the wedding!?

Japanese: ‥‥わたくしには わかりません‥‥
English: I don't understand at all...

Japanese: 男のくだらないプライド、 ってヤツなのかしら？
English: It was probably because of his worthless male pride.

Japanese: 藤見野と王都楼は、おたがいに 異常なライバル意識を抱いていた。
English: Juan and Matt were always fierce rivals.

Japanese: 婚約発表を待って、王都楼は 藤見野に事実をブチまけたの。
English: Matt waited for the wedding announcement and then unleashed the truth on Juan.

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野のダメージが、 一番大きいタイミングを狙って‥‥
English: He was aiming for when it would hurt Juan the most.

Japanese: 由利恵さんが‥‥ かわいそうです！
English: Poor Ms. Inpax!

Japanese: ‥‥話はまだ、終わらないわ。
English: ...That wasn't the end of it.

Japanese: あの日‥‥由利恵さんは、 やっぱり遺書をのこしていたの。
English: That day, I'm almost certain that Celeste left a suicide note behind.

Japanese: そこには‥‥王都楼の仕打ちが 詳しく書かれていたわ。
English: And in that note, she left a detailed account of Matt's various misdeeds, and...

Japanese: 彼に、二度までもキズつけられて ‥‥死を選ぶ、と。
English: So that she would never again be hurt by Matt, she chose... to die.

Japanese: 死体を発見した藤見野は‥‥ その遺書をかくしてしまった。
English: Then, when Juan discovered her body, he hid her note.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうして そんなことを！
English: B-But why would he do that!?

Japanese: カンタンなコトよ。
English: It's simple.

Japanese: その遺書は、王都楼　真悟に とって、決定的な弱みになるわ。
English: Juan realized that note was a powerful weapon against Matt.

Japanese: なにしろ、彼‥‥“春風のように サワヤカなアイツ”だから。
English: And it would be especially damaging to his "refreshing like a spring breeze" image.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。
English: In any case...

Japanese: プライドをキズつけられた 藤見野は、復讐をたくらんだの。
English: With his pride hurt, Juan sought revenge.

Japanese: 復讐‥‥。
English: Revenge... There's that word again.

Japanese: 藤見野はもちろん、 遺書を公表しようと考えていたわ。
English: Juan wanted to publicly disclose the contents of that suicide note.

Japanese: 王都楼に与えるダメージが、 一番大きいタイミングを狙って。
English: At a time that would cause Matt the most damage, of course.

Japanese: それが‥‥授賞式後の 記者会見だったんですか‥‥。
English: And that was at the press conference after the stage show...

Japanese: 私は、ゼンブ知っていたわ。 ‥‥藤見野から聞いたの。
English: I know all about it, because I heard it all from Juan.

Japanese: それを探るために‥‥ 彼に近づいたんだから。
English: It was so I could find out about all this that I drew close to Juan to begin with.

Japanese: サイテーの男たちでしょ？
English: They're quite a pair of hideous monsters, aren't they?

Japanese: 由利恵さんの死までも‥‥ もてあそんだ男たち。
English: Even Celeste's death was something for them to use in their game.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥藤見野の死体を 見つけたとき‥‥
English: That night, when I found Juan's body,

Japanese: 当然、犯人は 王都楼だと思ったわ。
English: it was only natural that I thought the murderer was Matt.

Japanese: あの２人‥‥おたがいのコト、 さぐりあっていたから。
English: Those two were always spying on one another, after all.

Japanese: 私、必死で由利恵さんの 遺書を探したわ。
English: As for me, I was frantically searching for Celeste's suicide note.

Japanese: できれば、公表されるまえに 始末してしまいたかった。
English: I wanted to destroy it before it ever went public.

Japanese: 燃やしてしまうつもりで、 ライターも用意していたわ‥‥。
English: I was going to burn it. I had even brought a lighter.
But...

Japanese: 遺書は見つからず‥‥、 私は、復讐を思いついたの。
English: I couldn't find the suicide note... And that's when revenge crossed my mind.

Japanese: いかにも私らしい ‥‥ズルい復讐を。
English: Yes, I was going to bring to them my own kind of cruel revenge.

Japanese: 由利恵さんは‥‥あの２人に 殺されたのよ。
English: Celeste was killed by those two monsters.

Japanese: 藤見野の死体にナイフを刺すとき ‥‥私は罪を感じなかったわ。
English: So when I stabbed Juan's body with that knife... I didn't feel a single shred of guilt.

Japanese: ‥‥これで私の話はおしまい。
English: And that's all I have to say.

Japanese: どうするの？　弁護士さん。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: これでも助けてやると言うの？ ‥‥あの男を。
English: Even knowing all this, are you still going to help that... "man"?

Japanese: ぼくは‥‥弁護士ですからね。
English: I... I'm a lawyer...

Japanese: そう‥‥。 イヤな商売ね、弁護士なんて。
English: I see. What a foul profession.

Japanese: ‥‥お話につきあってくれて、 ありがとうございました。
English: Thank you very much for your time and for talking with me.

Japanese: ‥‥いいわ。 どうせ、眠れそうにないから。
English: It was no big deal. I couldn't sleep anyway.

Japanese: （ぼくも‥‥眠れそうにないな）
English: (I can't sleep either... Not with Maya's situation... or with what I know now...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、探りあいは やめましょう、弁護士さん。
English: Please stop trying to ask me about such little things, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 私に聞きたいことが あるんじゃないですか？
English: Isn't there something else you should be asking me about?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: 事件当夜、ここには数百人が 招待されていたはずだ。
English: On that night, there must've been at least a few hundred people here.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、事情聴取も 終わったのだろうか。
English: Hmm... I guess the police are done with their questioning and investigating.

Japanese: ロビーは、やっと落ちつきを 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: It looks like things here in the lobby have finally calmed down.

Japanese: 取材に来た人たちの カメラだろう。
English: These cameras must belong to the press that came to cover the conference.

Japanese: どれも本格的なシロモノで、 ステージをねらっている。
English: They're all basically doing the same thing: staring at the empty stage.

Japanese: 吹き抜けのロビーは、 とにかく大きな空間に感じる。
English: A lobby with a grand staircase always feels enormous.

Japanese: 事件が起こって、かなり あわただしかったけど‥‥
English: When the murder happened, it was abuzz with people running back and forth...

Japanese: こうして落ちついてしまうと、 逆に不安になってくるな。
English: But now since things have quieted down, it actually feels more eerie than before.

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーの ステージだ。
English: It's the stage they were going to use for the post-ceremony stage show.

Japanese: ショーのあと、記者会見も 予定されていたというけど‥‥
English: After the show, there was supposed to be a press conference, but...

Japanese: でも、その記者会見‥‥
English: But that press conference...

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野さんが、オートロ さんを告発するつもりだったんだ。
English: It was really the victim, Juan Corrida, trying to disclose something about Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 同じヒーローなのに‥‥ どうして、そんなことを。
