English: But they're both heroes... Why would they do something so un-heroic?

Japanese: 見た目はヒーローだけど‥‥ 中身はニンゲンだからね。
English: They may look like heroes on the outside, but on the inside, they're only human.

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーと 記者会見のために用意されたイス。
English: Seats for the spectators of the post-ceremony show and press conference.

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 ムダになってしまった。
English: But it's too bad neither event is going to be held now.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってくださいよ！
English: Hey! Wait!

Japanese: なんだい！
English: What is it, whippersnapper!?

Japanese: アンタと関わると、ホンット ロクなコトがないョ！
English: All I know is nothing that has anything to do with you is ever good!

Japanese: さっき、なんだか知らんけど ヘンなキカイわたされてサ。
English: Like just now. I was handed this strange device for who knows what reason.

Japanese: こいつでホテルじゅう 調べろ、ってんだョ。
English: And I was told to use it to search the whole hotel.

Japanese: それ‥‥電波探知機ですね。 （イトノコ刑事、手作りの）
English: That's... the bug sweeper, isn't it? (The one Gumshoe made...)

Japanese: まあ、ミッちゃんのおねがいだから しかたないけどサ。
English: I don't know and frankly, I don't care. But the request came from Edgey-poo, so...
Edgeworth...?
And he said,

Japanese: “ウラむなら、青くとがった弁護士 をよろしく”
English: "If you feel angry, direct your anger at that unsophisticated lawyer."

Japanese: って言ってたから、
English: So, I'm going to feel free to direct all my anger towards you!

Japanese: オバチャン、ウラんじゃうよォ！
（‥‥ズルいヤツ）
English: (Ugh... Gee, thanks a bundle, Edgeworth! What a pal you are!)

Japanese: また、真宵さまと お食事をしたいです‥‥
English: I want to eat a meal with Mystic Maya again...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Yeah, me too...

Japanese: 真宵さまの食べっぷりを 見てると‥‥、
English: Whenever I watch Mystic Maya eat like she does...

Japanese: わたくしも“食べるぞ” という気分になります。
English: It makes me happy about eating, and then I can eat a lot.

Japanese: よし。
English: Well, then...

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、みんなに フルコースをごちそうするよ。
English: How about after we wrap up this case, we all go out for a huge twenty-course feast!

Japanese: そのコトバ、 忘れるんじゃないョ！
English: My mind's like a steel trap, so I'm going to hold you to that!

Japanese: （“お呼ばれ”する気だ！）
English: (I was NOT talking to you!)

Japanese: 料理はまだ かたづけられていない。
English: They haven't cleaned up all the food yet.

Japanese: 勝手にさわるんじゃないョ！
English: Don't you touch it without permission!

Japanese: オバチャン、今日はベントー箱 持ってきてるんだからネ。
English: I brought a lunch box today, so I could pack it.

Japanese: あ、でも‥‥もう、 ２日もたっていますよ？
English: Um... But... Hasn't the food been out for two days already?

Japanese: ハラん中、入れちまえば おんなじコトでショ！
English: When it's in your stomach, it's all the same!

Japanese: （さすが、宇宙人‥‥）
English: (Wow, the digestive powers of an alien are wondrous...)

Japanese: 授賞式が行われたステージだ。
English: The awards ceremony was held on that stage.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴージャスだったな、 さすがに。
English: It was really fabulous.

Japanese: ちょっと、あのサーカスを 思い出しました。
English: You just reminded me of the circus for a second.

Japanese: みなさん、おゲンキでしょうか？
English: I wonder if everyone is alright.

Japanese: （タチミ・サーカスは最近、 公演を再開したらしい）
English: (I heard that Berry Big Circus just recently started holding performances again...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ゲンキに やってると思うよ。
English: I'm sure they're all fine, Pearls.

Japanese: それにしても、 ゴウカなホテルだ。
English: This sure is one luxurious hotel.

Japanese: 何かにとりつかれたかのように、 ゴテゴテかざりつけされている。
English: Almost to the point of gaudy, with how it blends together everything "fancy" imaginable.

Japanese: （そういえば去年、あのボーイさん から暑中見舞いがきてたっけ）
English: (Speaking of fancy, didn't that bellboy give me something like that last year?)

Japanese: 大げさなロビーに通じる、 大げさなドアが見える。
English: There's a grand set of doors over there.

Japanese: ボーイにつれられた真宵ちゃんが 消えたドアだ。
English: It's the doors Maya followed the bellboy out of, only to disappear...

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき、ぼくたちが ついていれば‥‥
English: ...If only we had all gone together...

Japanese: わたくし、このような キレイなもの、初めて見ました。
English: That is such a beautiful sight.

Japanese: シャンデリア？
English: The chandelier?

Japanese: はい。‥‥こんな美しい 明かりの下で、
English: Yes. But I can't believe it...

Japanese: このようなヒドい事件が 起こるなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe that such a terrible murder happened under such beautiful lights...

Japanese: ショックです。
English: It's shocking.

Japanese: これ、ゼーッタイに ヒミツの話なんだけどサ！
English: This is absolutely top secret, so you had better keep it to yourselves!

Japanese: なんでも、プレゼントの中から 盗撮カメラが見つかったのョ！
English: I heard they found a spy camera hidden in one of the presents!

Japanese: そうみたいですね。
English: Hmm, very interesting.

Japanese: きっとアレだ。
English: I'm sure it was... you know...

Japanese: イサオちゃんのスキャンダルを 盗み見ようとしたんだョ！
English: It was to catch poor Juan in the middle of a scandalous "meeting"!

Japanese: “スキャンダル”‥‥ですか？
English: "Scandalous"...? What's that?

Japanese: 最近、イサオちゃんたら ウワサになってるからネ。
English: It means... Well, you know, that gossip that's been going around about my dear Juan.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 華宮　霧緒さんのコトですか。
English: Oh... You mean that thing about Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: （彼女がイサオさんに近づいたのは ‥‥目的があったんだけどな）
English: (But I'm sure she must've had some reason for getting close to Mr. Corrida...)

Japanese: オバチャン、知ってるョ。 ‥‥盗撮カメラのハンニン！
English: I'll let you in on another secret, young'un. I know who planted that spy camera!

Japanese: あの、ナマイキな大阪弁カメラ女。 まったく、あつかましいョ。
English: It was that obnoxious, puffy- haired photographer girl! The nerve of some people!

Japanese: 自分だけ、のぞこうなんてサ。 オバチャンも見たいッちゅう話ョ！
English: Spying on people by herself! As if I wouldn't want to see it for myself too!

Japanese: （‥‥うわあ、トンでもないコト 言ってるぞ。この宇宙人）
English: (Wow! The alien actually admitted her true intentions for a change!)

Japanese: 聞こえてるョ！
English: I don't know what you're thinking exactly, but I can bet that it's nothing good!!

Japanese: （言ってないのに‥‥）
English: (But I didn't say anything...)

Japanese: イサオちゃんと、 あのマネージャーだろ？
English: So you want to know about Juan and that manager, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ジッサイ、何か あったらしいョ、あのふたりは。
English: Actually, as I hear it, they were something of a refreshing pair, those two.

Japanese: そうなんですか。
English: Oh?

Japanese: いやー、イサオちゃん、 マネージャーにモテるネェ。
English: I tell you, Juan really welcomed that manager with open arms, I heard.

Japanese: “マネージャー”‥‥ですか？
English: That "manager"...? Who are you talking about?

Japanese: 知らないのかい？　イサオちゃんに ついていたマネージャー。
English: You don't know? That manager woman Juan had.

Japanese: ‥‥自殺しちまったケド。
English: It's a shame she killed herself, though...

Japanese: ああ。天野　由利恵さんですか？ ‥‥霧緒さんの先輩の。
English: Oh, you're talking about Ms. Celeste Inpax... Ms. Andrews' mentor, right?

Japanese: そう、それ！
English: Yes, yes, that's the one!

Japanese: そのユリエって子はネェ。
English: That Celeste girl.

Japanese: ケッコンするハズだったんだョ。
English: She was supposed to get married, you know.

Japanese: けけ、ケッコンって、 イサオさんと‥‥ですか？
English: M-Married? You mean... to Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: はー。マッタク、最近のコは なんにも知らないネェ。
English: *sigh* Really, you young kids today don't know anything, do you?

Japanese: ユリエが自殺したのは、 婚約発表の３日後だったんだョ。
English: That girl Celeste killed herself three days after their marriage announcement!

Japanese: （婚約発表の‥‥３日後！）
English: (Three days after their marriage announcement!? What in the...!?)

Japanese: 婚約した由利恵さんが、 どうして自殺したのですか！
English: Why would Ms. Inpax want to kill herself!? She was going to get married!

Japanese: ああ。そりゃカンタンなコトさネ。
English: Well, that's because...

Japanese: ‥‥捨てられちゃったんだョ。 イサオちゃんに。
English: she was thrown away, you see, by Juan.

Japanese: ええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: だ、だって‥‥おふたりは ‥‥婚約したのですよね？
English: B-But... They were going to get married, right? They promised each other, right?

Japanese: それはそれは大げさな 婚約発表だったョ。‥‥でも、
English: They held a grand announcement session... But...

Japanese: その３日後‥‥イサオちゃんは いきなり、それを取り消したんだ。
English: Three days later... Juan suddenly cancelled their marriage.

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントですか？
English: I-Is that true?

Japanese: 週刊誌に書いてあったョ。
English: It was in the weekly magazines.

Japanese: い、いったい、なぜですか！ どうして‥‥！
English: B-But why!? Why did he do that!?

Japanese: 週刊誌には書いてなかったネ。
English: That was not in the magazines, unfortunately.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: その晩だョ。ユリエっていう マネージャーが自殺したのは。
English: That night after Juan called off the wedding, that manager, Celeste, killed herself.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: How terrible...

Japanese: いったい‥‥ 何があったんでしょう‥‥。
English: I wonder what happened between those two...?

Japanese: アンタにやる情報なんて ないョ！
English: I don't have anything to say to delinquents like you!

Japanese: （うう‥‥見向きも しないぞ‥‥）
English: (Grrr... She's clamming up like the old clam she is...)

Japanese: おねがいします！ なんでもいいですから！
English: Please! Anything would be helpful!

Japanese: じゃ、オバチャンのスリーサイズ 教えてやろうか？
English: Well then, how about I tell you my measurements?

Japanese: ‥‥それはいいです。
English: ...Um... No, that's okay... Really.

Japanese: なるほどくん、人気が ないのですね‥‥
English: She really doesn't like you, does she Mr. Nick?

Japanese: メンボクない。
English: I know, I know...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 22 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: すっかり遅くなって しまいましたね‥‥
English: Wow, it's really getting late, isn't it Mr. Nick?

Japanese: もう、９時すぎだしね。
English: Yeah. It's past 9:00 PM already.

Japanese: でも‥‥まだ、明日の準備は 終わっていないよ。
English: But we still have some things to prepare for tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: （イサオさんがにぎっていた オートロさんの“ヒミツ”‥‥）
English: (There's still the matter of this "secret" Mr. Corrida held about Mr. Engarde...)

Japanese: （そして、霧緒さんの真意‥‥ これだけは知っておかないと！）
English: (And Ms. Andrews' real intentions. These are two things I must know tonight!)

Japanese: でも‥‥留置所の面会時間は もう、終わっているのですよね？
English: But aren't visiting hours over at the detention center?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん‥‥ なんとかするしかないなあ。
English: Hmm... I'm sure we'll think of something, Pearls. Don't you worry.

Japanese: まあ、愛らしい。
English: Aww, how lovely!

Japanese: クマさん、お花にかこまれて とてもうれしそうですね。
English: The teddy bear looks happy to be surrounded by so many beautiful flowers.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、やっぱり 好きみたいだな、ぬいぐるみ）
English: (Pearls really seems to like teddy bears a lot.)

Japanese: “王都楼さん江 英都プロダクション一同”‥‥
English: "To Mr. Engarde From the Global Studio Staff"

Japanese: 弁護士にも花輪が あればいいのにな。
English: Ah, it'd be nice if lawyers got flowers too. Something like,

Japanese: “成歩堂さん江 依頼人一同”‥‥とか。
English: "To Mr. Wright From All Your Grateful Clients".

Japanese: ドアに張り紙がある。 “王都楼　真悟様　控え室”
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Matt Engarde's Room".

Japanese: おばさま、おっしゃってましたね。 “今夜は、入ってはいけない”
English: The old lady told us about this, didn't she? That we can't go in this room tonight.

Japanese: （中では、捜査会議が開かれて いるんだろうな）
English: (I wonder if an investigation briefing is going on right now inside...)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事、ヌキで‥‥）
English: (Without Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: トイレなら、それぞれの 部屋にあるのだが、
English: There are toilets in each room in this hotel,

Japanese: このホテルでは、いろいろな イベントが開かれるので、
English: but since all sorts of events are held here,

Japanese: 泊まっている客以外の人たちが 使うのだろう。
English: there are bathrooms for people who aren't staying here to use as well.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくもそのひとりだけど。
English: Perfect for people like me, who can't afford a stay here in the first place...

Japanese: 向かいの花輪にくらべて、 さらにゴージャスだ。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: 事務所、レコード会社、 ファンクラブ、兄弟一同‥‥
English: Let's see... Record companies, fan clubs, company workers, family...

Japanese: 持って帰るのが タイヘンそうだな。
English: Carrying all these flowers home would be hard, I think...

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: （このドアの向こうで、 いったい何があったのか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder what EXACTLY happened in the room behind this door...?)

Japanese: （ぼくたちには、まだ わかっていないんだ‥‥）
English: (Even now, no one really has any idea...)

Japanese: ３月２２日　午後　９時１４分 藤見野　イサオの控え室
English: March 22, 9:14 PM Gatewater Hotel Corrida's Hotel Room

Japanese: ‥‥もう、９時をすぎて しまいましたね。
English: It's past 9:00 PM already, isn't it?

Japanese: みつるぎ検事さん‥‥真宵さまを 見つけてくださるでしょうか‥‥
English: I wonder... I wonder if Mr. Edgeworth has already found Mystic Maya...?

Japanese: （‥‥時間がなさすぎる。 まず、不可能だろうな）
English: (These things take time. I'd say probably not...)

Japanese: 警察は優秀だからね。 ‥‥キタイしていいと思うよ。
English: The police are professionals, Pearls. They'll find her, so don't you worry.

Japanese: それに明日は、無罪判決を 勝ちとる。‥‥問題ないよ。
English: And if we can win a not guilty verdict tomorrow... then everything will be OK.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね。
English: Y-You're right...

Japanese: 冷蔵庫の中は‥‥ いろいろ入ってるな。
English: There are all sorts of things in this refrigerator...

Japanese: ‥‥ニンジンジュースと‥‥ トマトジュースの空きビンです。
English: The carrot juice bottle and the tomato juice bottle are both empty...

Japanese: 空きビン‥‥？ （捨てるのがメンドウだったのか）
English: Empty...? (It's too much of a hassle to throw them away, I guess.)

Japanese: 野菜ジュースばっかりだね。 カラダに気をつかってたのかな。
English: They're all vegetable juices. I guess he must've been a real health nut.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。キムチとケチャップと イチゴジャムもありました。
English: ...Oh! There's a beet, some ketchup, and a bottle of strawberry jam too!

Japanese: ‥‥ただ赤い色が好きな だけかもしれないなあ。
English: Maybe red was his favorite color...?

Japanese: クマさんが ひしめいています！
English: Wow, there are a lot of bears!

Japanese: （目覚まし時計やら置物やら ぬいぐるみやらプラモデルやら）
English: (Alarm clock ones, collector's editions, stuffed teddies, plastic models...)

Japanese: （圧倒的な光景だな‥‥）
English: (It's pretty overwhelming... Is there a kind of bear he doesn't have...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ゴミ箱の中にも つっこまれてますけど‥‥。
English: There's even a few in the trash can...

Japanese: うーん‥‥本人は、あまり クマ好きじゃないのかも。
English: Yeah... I get the feeling maybe the guy didn't really like bears.

Japanese: クマさん、カワイソウです‥‥
English: Poor teddies...

Japanese: いやーホント、クマったもんだ。 ‥‥なーんて。
English: It's hard to "bear" with all these problems... Growl!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃらひゃら。あひゃ！ ひゃひゃひゃらあひゃあひゃ。ひゃ
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: （こないだの事件‥‥ある種、 トラウマになっちまったな）
English: (I don't think I want to "bear" with the trauma the last case caused me...)
What's wrong, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: もう！ こんなにちらかして‥‥
English: Hmph! It's so messy here!

Japanese: （ホント、キレイ好きだな 春美ちゃんは‥‥）
English: (Pearls really likes things neat and tidy, I take it...)

Japanese: それにしても、見たことのない キカイがいっぱいありますね。
English: There are a lot of electronic things in here that I've never seen before.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。 教えてください！
English: Hey, Mr. Nick! Tell me what they are, pretty please?

Japanese: そこにあるのがウデ時計だよ。 手首につけるんだ。
English: OK. That over there is a "watch". You wear it on your wrist.

Japanese: ‥‥知ってます。
English: I know what a watch is...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった。相手は 真宵ちゃんじゃないんだった）
English: (Oops. For a second there, I forgot I was talking with Pearls, not Maya...)

Japanese: トランクだ。いろんなものが ギュウと詰めこまれている。
English: It's a suitcase. There are so many things in it that I bet it barely closes.

Japanese: 着替えにドライヤー、 ヒゲそりに電卓、か‥‥。
English: There's his clothes, a dryer, an electric shaver, a calculator...

Japanese: 旅行に関係ないものが まじっていますね。
English: Do all stars pack too much stuff like Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: この中に‥‥トノサマンが 入っていたのですね。
English: So, the Nickel Samurai's costume was in here?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: そんなことまでして、 オートロさんの悪口を言おうと‥‥
English: I can't believe Mr. Corrida went so far, just to say bad things about Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥“記者会見”だけど。
English: W-Well, it was a "press conference", so he had to go in costume.

Japanese: オートロさんとイサオさんは、 友だちなのではないですか？
English: But weren't Mr. Engarde and Mr. Corrida friends?

Japanese: 友だちじゃないよ。 “ライバル”だから。
English: They weren't friends. They couldn't be friends because they were rivals.

Japanese: “強敵”と書いて“とも”と 読むのが、正しいライバルです！
English: So, a "rival" is someone who is a "strong enemy"!?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、アツいな‥‥）
English: (Pearls is really fired up over this... And I don't have an answer for her.)

Japanese: キレイなグラスだな。 中身は‥‥トマトジュースか。
English: It's a beautiful wine glass, and there's... tomato juice in it.

Japanese: わたくし、ニガテです ‥‥トマトジュース。
English: Ewww. Tomato juice. I don't really like it much.

Japanese: テーブルにボトルがあるね。 きっとあれだよ。
English: There's a bottle of it on the table over there. That's probably where this came from.

Japanese: これがウワサの “ベッド”というものですか‥‥。
English: So that's a bed...

Japanese: う、うん。ウワサのね。
English: Yup. It's big, but it's a bed.

Japanese: きゃあ！　なるほどくん！ ふかふかですッ！
English: Aaah! Mr. Nick! It's sooooo soft!

Japanese: （大事件みたいだ‥‥）
English: (How easily children are entertained.)

Japanese: イサオさん、 食事したみたいだね。
English: It looks like Mr. Corrida had dinner that night.

Japanese: このビン‥‥ トマトジュースです。
English: This bottle... It's tomato juice.

Japanese: （授賞式でゴチソウが たくさん並んでいたけど‥‥）
English: (We had a lot of food at the award show that night...)

Japanese: （スターって、 ハラがへるのかなあ）
English: (But I wonder if the stars had gone onstage after only eating a meager meal like this...)

Japanese: 化粧品のビンが 散乱している。
English: Bottles of cosmetics are scattered all over the floor.

Japanese: ‥‥ここで、イサオさんは 犯人と争ったのだろう。
English: This is probably where Mr. Corrida fought his assailant.

Japanese: この、ガラスのカケラが‥‥
English: These glass shards...

Japanese: 霧緒さんが落とした 花ビン、だね。
English: They're probably from the glass vase Ms. Andrews knocked over.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオさんを 殺害した犯人は‥‥
English: So the real person who killed Mr. Corrida was...

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門 ‥‥殺し屋なのですよね。
English: That assassin... Mr. Shelly de Killer, right?

Japanese: 霧緒さんが現場で拾ったカード‥‥ それが証拠らしいね。
English: And the card Ms. Andrews found at the crime scene seems to be proof of that.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。そうなると、 新しい問題が出てくる。
English: But if that's the case, then a new question comes to mind.

Japanese: “だれが虎狼死家に 殺害を依頼したか”‥‥？
English: Who was the one that hired De Killer to begin with? Who is his client?

Japanese: さつがいを‥‥いらい‥‥
English: You mean... Who asked for the murder...?

Japanese: 直接、手を下したワケじゃ ないけどね。
English: That person didn't want to dirty their own hands in blood,

Japanese: 殺害を依頼したヤツだって、 リッパな殺人者だ。
English: but whoever this client is, they're still a killer.

Japanese: いったい、だれが‥‥ コロシヤをたのんだのでしょう？
English: Who... Who could have hired the assassin?

Japanese: キリオさん‥‥なのでしょうか？
English: Do you think it was Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: （どうだろう‥‥）
English: (I wonder...)

Japanese: （仮に、彼女が 依頼人だとしたら‥‥）
English: (But if she was the client...)

Japanese: （自分で死体にナイフを刺して、 偽装工作なんかするだろうか？）
English: (then why go through the effort to stab the knife into the corpse herself...?)

Japanese: もし‥‥キリオさんでは ないとしたら‥‥
English: But if Ms. Andrews wasn't the client...

Japanese: ああ‥‥まさか‥‥
English: Then... No, it can't be...

Japanese: （‥‥王都楼　真悟‥‥？）
English: (...Matt Engarde himself...?)

Japanese: もし‥‥オートロさんが コロシヤにおねがいしたのなら‥‥
English: If Mr. Engarde really did hire the assassin...

Japanese: 彼は“無実”どころじゃない。 ‥‥まちがいなく、有罪だ。
English: Then he is not innocent at all. Far from it, he would be guilty of the crime.

Japanese: で、でも！　お、オートロさん じゃありませんよね！
English: B-But it can't be Mr. Engarde, right!?

Japanese: ‥‥だって、 初めてお会いしたとき‥‥
English: I mean, when we first talked with him...

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼さん。 ひとつだけ聞きます。
English: ...Mr. Engarde, I'd like to ask you one more question.

Japanese: あなたは、藤見野　イサオさんを 殺害しましたか？
English: Did you kill Mr. Juan Corrida?

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥ハッキリ答えましょう。
English: Alright. Just so we're clear, dude,

Japanese: ボク、だれも殺してません。 もちろん、藤見野　イサオも。
English: I didn't kill anyone, and that includes Juan Corrida, OK?

Japanese: （たしかに、サイコ・ロックは 見えなかった‥‥）
English: (I didn't see any Psyche-Locks at that time...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥何か引っかかる）
English: (Actually... That reminds me...)

Japanese: そ、それは、なんですか？ なるほどくん。
English: Did you remember something, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 今日の法廷で‥‥霧緒さんが、 気になることを言ったんだ。
English: Yeah, something Ms. Andrews said at the trial today. She said something interesting.

Japanese: あの‥‥“気になること” というのは‥‥？
English: Um... So what is this "interesting thing"?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。春美ちゃんは 聞いてないよね、もちろん。
English: Oh, that's right. You didn't hear it, did you Pearls?

Japanese: 藤見野は、《忍者ナンジャ》に すべてを賭けていました。
English: Juan had bet everything on the Jammin' Ninja this year.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、今年のグランプリを 逃したら、そのときは‥‥
English: And if he lost the Grand Prix...

Japanese: 王都楼自身を失脚させてやる。 ‥‥そう考えていたようです。
English: He was going to make sure Matt was going down with him. That's what he thought anyway.

Japanese: どうやら藤見野は‥‥
English: It looked like somehow, Juan had in his hands a secret so powerful...

Japanese: 王都楼の芸能生命を断つような ヒミツを‥‥にぎっていたのです！
English: that it would destroy Matt's acting career had it been revealed!

Japanese: オートロさんの ‥‥“ヒミツ”‥‥！
English: Mr. Engarde's "secret"...!?

Japanese: い、いったいそれは、 どのような‥‥？
English: Wh-What is this secret...?

Japanese: それは、まだわからない。
English: I don't know yet.

Japanese: とにかくイサオさんは、その ヒミツを暴露しようとしていた。
English: But for now, let's think about it this way. Mr. Corrida was going to reveal this "secret".

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。
English: That means...

Japanese: オートロさんには、リッパな “動機”があることになるんだ。
English: Mr. Engarde had plenty of motive to have Mr. Corrida silenced.

Japanese: （やっぱり、会わなければ ならないな‥‥王都楼さんに）
English: (Which means we have to meet with Mr. Engarde. There's no way around it now.)

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、信じています。 弁護士さんのチカラを。
English: I... I believe in your lawyer powers, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: なるほどくんなら‥‥きっと、 キセキを起こしてくれますよね。
English: I know you can bring us a miracle.

Japanese: （‥‥オートロさんの無罪判決が “キセキ”だと思ってるのか）
English: (I'm guessing by "miracle", she means a not guilty verdict...)

Japanese: （まちがっては‥‥いないな）
English: (And she wouldn't be wrong either. It will take a miracle to pull this one off.)

Japanese: どうして‥‥ユーカイ犯は、 真宵さまを‥‥
English: Why... Why did the kidnapper have to take Mystic Maya...?

Japanese: どうせさらうのなら、 わ、わたくしを‥‥！
English: Why couldn't he have taken me instead!?

Japanese: ダメだよ。そんなコトを 言っちゃあ。
English: Stop it, Pearls! Don't say things like that!

Japanese: ‥‥もしそんなことになったら、 真宵ちゃんが苦しむんだよ。
English: If that had happened, then Maya would be hurting, I'm sure of it.

Japanese: 今の春美ちゃんみたいに。
English: Just like how you are hurting now, Pearls.

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥。
English: Y-You're right...

Japanese: 今回の事件で‥‥わたくし、 いろいろなコトを知りました。
English: I've learned a lot of things during this case.

Japanese: だれかの生活をのぞくために、 このようなキカイがあるなんて。
English: For example, if you want to spy on someone, there are devices like this you can use.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、のぞくための機械じゃ ないんだけど。
English: Well, it's not really a device for the purpose of spying.

Japanese: その使い道を決めるのは 人間だからね。
English: The way any device is used is decided by the people who use them.

Japanese: クマさんが‥‥かわいそうです。
English: Poor teddy... I feel bad for it.

Japanese: 犯人のいやしい目的のために、 め、目を‥‥
English: Its eye was... It was... And all for the criminal's mean plan...

Japanese: （このクマ‥‥ 海外の高級ブランド、か）
English: (This bear... It's an expensive luxury brand from overseas.)

Japanese: 手がかりになってくれれば いいんだけどね。
English: But it's OK, Pearls. Now it's helping us by giving us a clue...

Japanese: ‥‥犯人に仕返しができるから。
English: It's telling us who the real culprit is so we can get some justice.

Japanese: あの。わたくし、お役に 立ちたいのはやまやまなのですが。
English: Um, there are still so many things in this world that I don't understand yet.

Japanese: ‥‥修行が足りません。まだ。
English: ...Honestly, I still don't have enough training.

Japanese: い、いいよ。心当たりが ないだけなんだから。
English: Ah, no, it's OK. I don't have any ideas myself...

Japanese: すみません、すみません‥‥
English: I'm sorry. I'm really sorry!

Japanese: わたくし、こんなに ソーメン好きな殿方、初めてです。
English: This is the first time I've ever met someone who loves to eat instant noodles.

Japanese: （好きで食べてるワケでは ないと思うけど‥‥）
English: (Something tells me Gumshoe isn't eating them because he genuinely likes them, but...)

Japanese: 真宵さまのために、こんなに がんばってくださって‥‥
English: He's working so hard, all for Mystic Maya's sake...

Japanese: わたくし、おこづかいで ソーメンをプレゼントしますね！
English: I'm going to get him lots and lots of instant noodles as a thank you present!

Japanese: わたくしが、もっとキチンと 修行をしていれば‥‥
English: If only I had studied harder during my training...

Japanese: もっと、なるほどくんの おチカラになれたのに。
English: I could be a bigger help to you, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: お、落ちこまないで。 春美ちゃんは、じゅうぶん‥‥
English: Ah, don't worry about it. Really. Pearls, you're doing a great job...

Japanese: なるほどくん！　この近くに、 滝はありませんか！
English: Mr. Nick! Is there a waterfall around here!?

Japanese: わたくし、モーレツに 滝に打たれたい気分です！
English: I feel that I must be made new under the rushing spring waters!

Japanese: ごめん。‥‥この辺には、 湖しかないんだ。
English: Sorry, Pearls... The only body of water around here is a lake.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Oh... Thanks anyway...
One more day...

Japanese: うん。‥‥裁判が終われば、 帰ってくるよ。
English: Yeah... If we can bring the trial to its end, she'll be back. Don't worry.

Japanese: わたくし、もう泣きません。
English: I decided I won't cry anymore, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: だって‥‥なるほどくんが ガマンしているのですから。
English: You're so brave and holding strong for Mystic Maya, so I have to do the same.

Japanese: あの‥‥おばさまは、 本当に地球人なのでしょうか。
English: Um... I was wondering if that old lady is really a person from Earth.

Japanese: もしかしたら、じつは とおい宇宙のかなたから‥‥
English: I mean, maybe she's really from waaaaaay out in outer space...

Japanese: うん。‥‥じつは、ぼくも 同じことを考えていたよ。
English: Yeah, actually, I was thinking the same exact thing. She's too strange to be from Earth.

Japanese: ‥‥スターの座、というのは、 そんなに大切なものなのですか？
English: Um... Is it really that important to be the Master of all the Stars?

Japanese: お友だちの弱みをニギろうとしたり ‥‥それをバクロしようとしたり。
English: So important that you try to find your friends' weaknesses, and then expose them...?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくにはわからないよ。
English: I... I wouldn't know...

Japanese: わ、わたくしたちは‥‥ 信用していいのでしょうか？
English: I-Is it really alright for us to trust him?

Japanese: このかたを‥‥ オートロさんを‥‥。
English: This person... Mr. Engarde...?

Japanese: （こ、答えることができない）
English: (I-I don't know... I can't even answer that for myself.)

Japanese: （‥‥やはり‥‥ もう一度、会わなければ！）
English: (We have to go see him one more time! That's the only way to know for sure!)

Japanese: キリオさんは‥‥どうして、 オートロさんに罪を着せようと？
English: Why did Ms. Andrews... Why did she frame Mr. Engarde, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （彼女は、オートロさんに 悪意がある‥‥）
English: (She has some sort of grudge against Mr. Engarde...)

Japanese: （それは‥‥なぜか？ まだ、わかっていない‥‥）
English: (But why is that? That's something we still have to figure out...)

Japanese: 彼女に‥‥聞いてみないとね。
English: Let's go ask her, OK?

Japanese: キリオさんの先輩で‥‥ じさつ‥‥されたのですよね。
English: This person was Ms. Andrews' mentor... And she... killed herself, right?

Japanese: （遺書は、イサオさんが かくしてしまった‥‥）
English: (Right. And Mr. Corrida hid Ms. Inpax's suicide note...)

Japanese: （それを取りもどすため、霧緒さん はイサオさんに近づいた‥‥）
English: (So to get it back, Ms. Andrews got close to Mr. Corrida...)

Japanese: （でも、ケッキョク‥‥ その遺書は、どこにあるんだ？）
English: (But the thing is, where is that suicide note now?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 22 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、 空気がピリピリしています。
English: It feels sort of tense in here, doesn't it Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （ホントだ。 何かあったのかな‥‥？）
English: (Yeah, it does. I wonder if something happened...?)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかきみは、王都楼の 弁護士だったね？
English: You're Mr. Engarde's lawyer, right?

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Ah, yes sir.

Japanese: ワレワレは、やっと見つけたんだ。 ‥‥決定的な証人を。
English: Well, we've finally found just the person we've been looking for. A real decisive witness.

Japanese: “決定的な”‥‥って、 オートロさんの事件ですか？
English: A "decisive witness"? You mean for Mr. Engarde's case?

Japanese: 今、調書を取り直しているんだ。 ‥‥さすが、御剣検事だねえ。
English: We're taking the witness' statement right now. Gotta hand it to Mr. Edgeworth.
(What's Edgeworth up to now...?)

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥どなたですか？ 証人というのは。
English: Wh-Who is this witness?

Japanese: 弁護士クンも、よく知っている 人物だよ。
English: I think you know this person quite well, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: な、なるほどくん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: （誘拐犯に、オートロさん‥‥ どっちも絶体絶命、か！）
English: (Between the kidnapper's demand and now this... I can't see any way to win here!)

Japanese: あ、それから‥‥。
English: Oh, yeah...

Japanese: 御剣検事から たのまれたコトがあるんだ。
English: Mr. Edgeworth wanted me to tell you something.

Japanese: 御剣から‥‥？
English: He did?

Japanese: 留置所の面会時間は とっくに終わっているけど‥‥、
English: Even though visiting hours are long over at the detention center,

Japanese: トクベツに、面会を許可してやって ほしい、‥‥ってね。
English: he wanted me to grant you special permission, so there you go.

Japanese: えっ！
English: Eh!?

Japanese: ワシのほうから電話を 入れておくから、
English: I've already called them so they know.

Japanese: ま。好きなだけハナシを 聞くといいよ。
English: Go on, go talk to your heart's content.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you very much.

Japanese: よかったですね、なるほどくん。
English: This is such good news, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: （“好きなだけハナシを聞け” ‥‥か）
English: ("Go talk to your heart's content"?)

Japanese: （どうやら警察は、明日の審理に かなり自信があるみたいだ‥‥）
English: (It sounds like the police are pretty sure they have tomorrow's trial in the bag.)

Japanese: ‥‥ワシの作ったマスコット、 “タイホくん”だよ。
English: This mascot here is the "Blue Badger". I made him, you know.

Japanese: ついに明日、警視総監どのに お見せするコトになったんだ。
English: I'm finally gonna show him off to the Chief of Police tomorrow.

Japanese: へえ。‥‥それはスゴい。
English: Wow, that's really something.

Japanese: ‥‥ここまでたどりつくまでに、 いくら使ったことか‥‥。
English: You have no idea how much time and effort I invested into him to get this far...

Japanese: だが、ワシの野望は、 まだまだ終わらんよ。
English: But my hopes for this little guy go far beyond just this precinct, yessiree.

Japanese: 最終的には、各署にひとりずつ、 着ぐるみ警官を配備するつもりさ。
English: I'll get him deployed to every precinct in the nation. Just you wait.

Japanese: おしごと、がんばってください。
English: I wish you luck, sir.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “‥‥どうやら、オレの正体を 明かすときが来たようだな‥‥”
English: "It seems as though my true face has been revealed..."

Japanese: “あるときは、世界と取り引き する、エリートビジネスマン！”
English: "I was an elite businessman who made deals with the entire world!"

Japanese: “あるときは、巨大コンツェルンの 若き二代目社長！”
English: "I was the young successor and CEO of a colossal corporate conglomeration!"

Japanese: “その正体は‥‥ ただのオトリ捜査官だッ！”
English: "But my real self is that of a regular everyday policeman!"

Japanese: イメージ・トレーニングなんか してないで、捜査に行けよ！
English: Stop doing image training, and get yourself out there in the field!!

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！　“ヒーロー殺人事件 は、殺し屋のシワザか”‥‥
English: Whaaat!? "The real killer in the Corrida murder is an assassin..."!?

Japanese: あの報告書、ホントだったのか。 冗談だと思って捨てちまったぞ！
English: This must be someone's idea of a joke. I can't believe this garbage!

Japanese: （‥‥もっと部下を 信じてやれよ！）
English: (Have a little more faith in your own subordinates!)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I expected.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 22 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: あっ！　成歩堂さん！ 助けてくださいッ！
English: Oh! Mr. Wright! Please, you have to help me!

Japanese: （この声は‥‥）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: に、荷星さん！
English: M-Mr. Powers!

Japanese: どうされましたか？ こんなところで‥‥
English: What happened? Why are you here?

Japanese: い、いや、ボク‥‥ なんか、捕まっちゃって。
English: I-I... Uh, you see, I got roped into this somehow...

Japanese: えええッ！
English: What??

Japanese: ボク、明日の法廷で 証言することになったんですよ。
English: And now I'm going to testify at tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: （決定的な証人‥‥って、 荷星さんだったのか‥‥）
English: (So the decisive witness... is Mr. Powers?)

Japanese: さっきまで刑事さんとハナシを して、帰ろうと思ったら‥‥
English: I was talking with a detective until a little while ago, and I was on my way home...

Japanese: “被疑者、身柄をカクホー！”と イキナリ、こう来たわけですよ。
English: When all of a sudden, "You there! You're under arrest!" And I was brought back here.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: O-Oh.

Japanese: なんか、“カオもカッコウも アヤシイ！”‥‥なんて言われて。
English: They said my face and whole self in general looked "suspicious" or something.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥たしかにアヤシイ。 ‥‥カオもカッコウも）
English: (Hmm... Well, I guess I can see how they thought you looked suspicious...)

Japanese: ううう‥‥ただの 体操のおニイさんなのに。
English: *sigh* I'm just a normal guy on an exercise show for kids... Is that a crime??

Japanese: なるほどくん！　お電話が‥‥
English: Mr. Nick! Your phone!

Japanese: お。トノサマンのテーマですね！
English: Hey, that's the Steel Samurai theme song, isn't it!?

Japanese: こうして聞いてみると‥‥
English: I don't like the sound of this ringtone right now...

Japanese: ‥‥なんて不吉な メロディなんだろう。
English: It sounds kind of ominous.

Japanese: きょ‥‥、恐縮です。
English: Y-Yeah, I know.

Japanese: もしもし
English: Hello--

Japanese: タイヘンッス！
English: We're in trouble now, pal!

Japanese: すぐ‥‥ すぐ、事務所にもどるッス！
English: I'll... I'll be back at the office really soon!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか
English: Wh-What's wrong!?

Japanese: とんでもないコトに なっちまったッス！
English: Something really unexpected just happened!

Japanese: 御剣検事が、その‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth... He...
(Edgeworth...?)

Japanese: とにかく、早く帰ってくるッス！
English: Anyway, hurry up and get back to the office, pal!

Japanese: もう、何がなんだかわかんねッス！ ダメッス！　終わりッス！自分は
English: I don't know what's going on anymore! It's no good! The end! I --

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。切れちまったぞ。
English: ...Hello? He got cut off.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうかしましたか？ なるほどくん！
English: Wh-What's going on, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: 事務所に帰ってこいって。
English: Gumshoe said we need to go back to the office right away.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ 早く、まいりましょう！
English: Th-Then we should hurry back!

Japanese: ‥‥帰るのがコワい。
English: ...I'm scared to go back.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですかッ！
English: What are you talking about!? Mr. Nick! Pull yourself together!

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: いいニュースだといいですね！
English: Maybe it'll be good news!

Japanese: （そんなワケ、ないって）
English: (Somehow, I doubt that...)

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “‥‥ゴメン。オレ、みんなに ウソついてたんだ‥‥”
English: "...I... I'm sorry, everyone. I've lied to you all..."

Japanese: “オレ、刑事なんかじゃない。 じつは‥‥じつは‥‥”
English: "I... I'm not a detective. I'm really... I'm really..."

Japanese: “ナゾのエスニック・ヒーロー、 オニャンコポンだったんだ！”
English: "I'm really Global Hero Onyankopon!"

Japanese: ナニが真実なのか わからなくなってきたぞ‥‥
English: I think he's lost his grip on what's real and what isn't...

Japanese: “証言”って‥‥ 荷星さん、何を話すんですか？
English: So, about this "testimony" you're giving. What are you going to talk about?

Japanese: イヤ、よく わからないんですけど‥‥
English: Ah, I really don't know yet...

Japanese: なんかボク、ダイジなものを 見ちゃったみたいなんですよ。
English: But it sounds like I saw something pretty important from what they tell me.

Japanese: ダイジなもの‥‥？
English: You saw something important? What was it?

Japanese: あ。でも‥‥刑事さんに 言われてるんです。
English: Ah, well, the detective told me not to talk about it.

Japanese: “ゼッタイにしゃべるな。 特にあの弁護士には”って。
English: "You can't tell anyone, and especially not that lawyer," he said.

Japanese: “あの弁護士”というのは‥‥
English: Who do you think is "that lawyer" the detective was talking about...?

Japanese: まあ、ぼくだろうね。
English: I'm going to take a wild guess and say it's me.

Japanese: ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: ...Y-Yeah, you got it.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 世界で、わたくしと真宵さまだけは なるほどくんの味方です！
English: Mystic Maya and myself are your only two allies in this whole world, but it's alright!

Japanese: （少ないな‥‥ミカタ）
English: (Ouch... I don't really have a lot of friends, do I...?)

Japanese: オートロくん‥‥きっと、 ダメージが大きいですね。
English: This is going to do a lot of damage to Matt, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥彼、春風のような サワヤカさがウリだから。
English: Because he's got that "refreshing like a spring breeze" image going.

Japanese: ホントのところは どうなんですか？
English: But what is he really like?

Japanese: そうですねえ。女性にはちょっと だらしないというか‥‥
English: Well, let's see. Matt's always been kind of a "player" with women...

Japanese: まわりに寄ってくる方を、 その‥‥こばまないんですよ。
English: He would never really turn a pretty face away, if you know what I mean...

Japanese: “ゲームだ”って 割り切ってですね‥‥
English: He'd always say, "It's just a game" to justify himself.

Japanese: まあっ！ ゆる、ユルせません！
English: What!? H-How horrible! That's unforgivable!

Japanese: いてて。 ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: Oww. S-Sorry. Didn't mean to offend you.

Japanese: “思いどおりにならなかった オンナは１人だけだ”って‥‥
English: But you know, he said once that,

Japanese: そんなコト、言ってましたね。
English: "There's only one person in the world who won't swoon over me."

Japanese: “１人だけ”‥‥。 それ、だれですか？
English: One person who wouldn't swoon over him?

Japanese: マネージャーさんですよ。 華宮　霧緒さん。
English: His manager, you know. Ms. Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: （荷星さん‥‥なんだか、 妙にイキイキしてきたぞ‥‥）
English: (Why is Mr. Powers suddenly looking kind of... energetic?)

Japanese: いやぁ。ボク、ウワサ話には 目がないんですよ。
English: Ah, you see, I'm actually a sucker for gossip.

Japanese: なんか芸能界って、キラびやかな イメージがあるじゃないですか。
English: I mean, celebrities and their world have this dazzling sort of image, right?

Japanese: “あるじゃないですか”って、 荷星さんもそこにいるでしょう？
English: A dazzling sort of image? But aren't you a part of that dazzle, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: いや、ボクなんて。毛むくじゃらで 汗クサいキンニク野郎ですから。
English: No, I'm more of a hairy, sweaty, smelly, brutish kind of guy, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、いいんです。
English: But it's OK, really.

Japanese: 身のまわりで、スターたちの ゴシップがナマで聞けますからね。
English: I get to hear plenty of gossip about a lot of the other stars around me as things happen.

Japanese: まあ‥‥そうですね。
English: Well, that's true.

Japanese: あ、ホラ！　知ってますか？ 霧緒さんのセンパイの自殺‥‥
English: Oh, hey! So did you hear about this yet? About Ms. Andrews' mentor and her suicide...?

Japanese: 由利恵さん、ですか？
English: You mean Ms. Inpax?

Japanese: たしか‥‥イサオさんとの 結婚がダメになった、とか。
English: We heard something about how her wedding was cancelled...

Japanese: そう、それです！
English: Yeah, that's what I'm talking about!

Japanese: ボクなりにね。考えてみたんです！ あの、ナゾの死について！
English: I thought about it a little the other day! About that mysterious death!

Japanese: ね。ね。成歩堂さん！ 聞いてくれますか！
English: Hey, Mr. Wright! Why don't you ask me about that!? Go on, go ahead!

Japanese: （荷星さん‥‥なんだか、 ハダがツヤツヤしてきたぞ‥‥）
English: (Mr. Powers is so charged up his skin is practically glowing with electricity...)

Japanese: 知ってますか？
English: Hey, so have you heard this?

Japanese: 由利恵さん、遺書を のこしていたんですよ！
English: Celeste left a suicide note!

Japanese: それを、イサオさんが かくしちゃった、って‥‥
English: And they say that Juan went and hid it...

Japanese: 法廷で聞きましたよ。
English: We heard about that in court today.

Japanese: （“証拠はない”って 言ってたけど‥‥）
English: (But there wasn't any actual proof that she had left a note...)

Japanese: ボク、思うんですよ。
English: Well, this is what I think.

Japanese: 遺書の中に、ツゴウの悪いコトが 書いてあったんです！
English: I think that something bad was written on that note.

Japanese: ‥‥イサオさんにとって。
English: Something bad for Juan, that is.

Japanese: ツゴウの悪いコト‥‥
English: Something bad for Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: どうして そう思うんですか？
English: Why do you figure so?

Japanese: 由利恵さん、亡くなる前に トモダチに相談してたんですよ。
English: Well, before she died, Celeste talked with a few of her friends.

Japanese: “ワルいオトコに つかまってしまった”って‥‥
English: And she said, "It looks like I may have been caught by an insidious man..."

Japanese: “ワルいオトコ”‥‥ それがイサオさんだった、と？
English: An "insidious man"...? Did she mean Mr. Corrida by that?

Japanese: だって、他に 考えられないでしょう？
English: Well, there's no one else that fits the bill, right?

Japanese: ヒトの遺書をかくしちゃう 理由なんて！
English: And that would be reason enough for him to hide the suicide note!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥ 参考になりました。
English: I see... Well, that's some good info. Thank you.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です！
English: Y-You're welcome!

Japanese: （王都楼さんと霧緒さん‥‥ ふたりは今、留置所にいる）
English: (Mr. Engarde and Ms. Andrews; they're both at the detention center right now.)

Japanese: （何かまだ、ぼくが知らない ことがあるはずだ‥‥）
English: (There are still things I don't understand or know about, I'm sure...)

Japanese: （すべて聞き出すんだ‥‥ あのふたりから‥‥！）
English: (I have to get the two of them to tell me everything!)

Japanese: いやー、そいつには お世話になりました。
English: Ah, I really owe you one.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんたちがいなかったら ボクはどうなっていたことか‥‥
English: If it wasn't for you guys, I don't know what would've happened.

Japanese: 本当に、大変なおシゴトだと 思いますよ。
English: What you did... It was a real tight spot I was in, and you fought so hard to get me out.

Japanese: ボク、何があっても 一生忘れませんから。
English: I don't care what happens. I'll never forget everything you've done for me.

Japanese: あの‥‥ユーカイ犯は、 あれから何か‥‥？
English: Um... That kidnapper... Has he said anything since then...?

Japanese: それが‥‥連絡が とれないんですよ。
English: Well... He tried, but there was a lot of interference.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: （あの電波障害‥‥）
English: (That radio interference...)

Japanese: （まさか、盗撮カメラだとは 気づかなかったな‥‥）
English: (Who would've thought it was caused by a spy camera?)

Japanese: アコガレちゃいますよねえ。 ‥‥まっかなギター。
English: The Jammin' Ninja made me want a bright red guitar, you know.

Japanese: ボクもね。マネして 買ってみたんですよ、ギター。
English: I wanted to be cool like him, so I went out and bought one.

Japanese: はあ‥‥そうなんですか。
English: Oh... That's nice...

Japanese: いやー、ゼンゼン音が出なくて。
English: Ah, but you don't understand. I can't get a single sound to come out of it.

Japanese: ふみつぶしちゃいましたね。 ‥‥買った次の日に。
English: Because I sort of tripped on it and crushed it... the day after I bought it.

Japanese: 霧緒さんが、オートロくんに ツミを着せようなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe Ms. Andrews tried to frame Matt...

Japanese: あの２人の関係‥‥どうも、 ナゾめいてますよねえ‥‥。
English: Those two have such a strange relationship with each other, don't they?

Japanese: （どうやら、荷星さんは その答えを知らないみたいだ）
English: (Looks like Mr. Powers is just as in the dark about things as I am.)

Japanese: （ぼく自身で見つけだすしか ないな‥‥ふたりに会って）
English: (I guess I don't have a choice other than to go talk to the two of them myself.)

Japanese: あ。その記事！ “ゴシップランド”の‥‥？
English: Hey! That article! Is that from "Gossip Land"?

Japanese: え、ええ。 ‥‥そんな雑誌みたいですね。
English: Y-Yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: いやぁ、ボクもダイスキ なんですよ。この雑誌。
English: Ah, you too? I absolutely love this magazine!
Um... "You too"...?

Japanese: あ。いっぺん、ボクのコトも のったことがあるんですよ！
English: Oh, they wrote a little something about me once!

Japanese: “深夜のトノサマン、ギョーザを 食い逃げ？”‥‥って。
English: "Steel Samurai's Midnight Dine and Dash".

Japanese: はあ‥‥ギョーザを。
English: Dine and dash...?

Japanese: ボク、その雑誌、１０さつも 買っちゃいましたよ。
English: I bought ten copies of that issue, you know.

Japanese: （できれば、この記事について 話してほしかった‥‥）
English: (Do you think we could somehow get back to THIS article...?)

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thanks for taking the time to take it out and show it to me,

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: but I'm really sorry, I don't know a thing about it.

Japanese: あの。真宵さんの 妹さんですか？
English: So are you Maya's little sister?

Japanese: いえ。ちがいます。
English: I'm sorry, but no.

Japanese: あ。じゃあ、成歩堂さんの 妹さん。
English: Oh, then you must be Mr. Wright's little sister!

Japanese: そうではありませんけど。
English: Again, I'm sorry, but...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、じゃあ どなたの妹さんなんですか？
English: Then... Whose little sister are you?

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥ひとりっ子なんです。
English: I'm sorry, but I'm an only child.

Japanese: あ、そうなんですか。 ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: Oh, I see... S-Sorry.

Japanese: （おたがいに恐縮しあっている）
English: (Pearl looks so sad... She looks really sorry too...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ブジ 帰ってきますよ。
English: I... I'm sure he won't hurt her and he'll let her go soon...

Japanese: ええ‥‥。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。 あなたならだいじょうぶです！
English: Mr. Wright. I believe in you. You will save her!

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks.

Japanese: そんなにコワいんですかねえ、 このカオ。
English: Hey, is my face really all that scary?

Japanese: 子どもたちはだれも 近よって来ないし‥‥
English: Kids won't come anywhere near me...

Japanese: ボクがアタマをかくと、 みなさんビクッとするし‥‥
English: But when I cover my face, they're all shocked because then they figure out who I am.

Japanese: わたくしは、とても やさしいおカオだと思います。
English: I think your face makes you look like a very kind person!

Japanese: （荷星さん、 ナミダぐんじゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (Aww... Mr. Powers is getting teary eyed.)

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、こんなところで また会うなんて。
English: I never would've thought I'd see her here of all places.

Japanese: この前の事件では、ヒドい目に あいましたからねえ。
English: The last time we saw each other was during that really bad incident... You know...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、今ではもう いい思い出ですけど。
English: I mean, when I look back now, I think of the good things, so it's not so bad, I guess.

Japanese: その思い出を、もう一度 味わってるんですよ‥‥ぼく。
English: Yeah, I would love to go back in time and relive a few of those good things... *chuckle*

Japanese: それは‥‥笑いゴトじゃ ないですねえ。
English: Th-That's not funny...

Japanese: （ホントに‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I guess not...)

Japanese: オートロくん、ボクの 後輩なんですけどね。
English: Matt's younger than me,

Japanese: やっぱり、出てますよ。 スターのオーラが。
English: but you can practically see his star potential.

Japanese: すたーのおおら‥‥ですか。
English: His star po-ten-shial...?

Japanese: サインもらっちゃいましたもん、 ボク。
English: I got his autograph the other day.

Japanese: ‥‥それはちょっと、 カッコわるいですね。
English: ...Feels kind of wrong now, doesn't it?

Japanese: オートロくんがイサオくんを‥‥ そんなコト、ボクは信じません！
English: I don't care what people say! Matt didn't kill Juan! I know he didn't!

Japanese: デビューが近かったんですよ。 オートロくんと。
English: He debuted around the same time as Matt in everything, you know.

Japanese: そうなのですか。
English: Really?

Japanese: “スター・のどじまん大会”やら “スター・水泳大会”やら‥‥
English: It started out small... First it was singing contests, then swimming competitions...

Japanese: “スター・ボウリング大会”やら “スター・大忘年会”やら‥‥
English: Then it was bowling tournaments, and then it was who could throw the best

Japanese: ナニかと張り合っていましたねえ。
English: New Year's parties... Juan was always trying to one-up Matt.

Japanese: 最近は、だんだん エスカレートしてきて‥‥
English: But lately, those two were escalating to more and more dangerous things...

Japanese: タイヘンなコトになるんじゃないか って、シンパイしてたんですよ。
English: I thought that no good would come of it all, so I began to worry.

Japanese: （そしてついに‥‥殺人事件が 起こってしまったワケか）
English: (Too bad Juan's story ended so soon...)

Japanese: 霧緒さんですね！ オートロくんのマネージャー。
English: Hey, that's Ms. Andrews! She's Matt's manager.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、ちょっとだけ アコガレてたりするんですよ‥‥。
English: Actually, I was interested in her for a little bit. Just a little...

Japanese: （こういうタイプが スキなのか‥‥荷星さん）
English: (Hmm... So Mr. Powers likes this type of woman...)

Japanese: 何かご存じないですか？ ‥‥霧緒さんのこと。
English: What do you know about Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: そうですね。その。 ご存じないワケではないような‥‥
English: Well, see... Here's the thing. I don't really KNOW her know her... you know?

Japanese: あ。なんでしたら、 詳しくお話ししましょうか！
English: Ah! If you're interested, I can give you a little bit of the details!

Japanese: （なんか、ウレシそうだな‥‥）
English: (He's so happy, he looks like a lion that's just found his next meal...)

Japanese: イサオさんのマネージャー ‥‥ですよね。
English: That's Juan's former manager, right?

Japanese: 由利恵さん。ムカシはボクたちの 英都プロにいたんですけどねえ‥‥
English: Celeste Inpax... She was with Global Studios for a while way back when...

Japanese: いろいろあって、イサオくんのいる 光映プロに移ったんですよ。
English: But some things happened and she ended up moving over to Worldwide Studios.

Japanese: “いろいろ”‥‥ですか？
English: "Some things"? As in?

Japanese: 詳しいことを知ってるヒト‥‥ いないんですよねえ。
English: Well, there's no one left around who knows the details, you know.

Japanese: ウワサばっかりで。
English: Only rumors are left now.

Japanese: ううう‥‥どうも そのようなかたは。
English: Umm... I'm honored you're asking me,

Japanese: 恐縮ですが。その。 ‥‥特に何も言うことは。ええ。
English: I don't know anything special about this person... Umm... Sorry...

Japanese: そ、そこまで恐縮しなくても。
English: Ah, it's OK. You don't have to apologize so much.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: March 22 Police Station Criminal Affairs Dept.

Japanese: どうする気だ？　成歩堂。 弁護の方針を変えるのなら‥‥
English: Well, Wright? What are you going to do? If you plan on changing your defense...

Japanese: だ‥‥ダメです！
English: N...No!

Japanese: それだけは‥‥ダメ‥‥
English: We can't do that...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだった。人質‥‥か。
English: That's right... He's holding Maya hostage...

Japanese: ぼくは‥‥どうしたらいいんだ‥‥
English: What... What should I do...?

Japanese: 私の知ったことではないな。
English: That's not something I can answer for you.

Japanese: み‥‥みつるぎ検事さん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。それは、 キミにしか決めることはできない。
English: Wright... Only you can decide where to go from here.

Japanese: １年前の事件で、私は“検事”と いうものが、わからなくなった。
English: One year ago... At that time, I didn't truly understand what a "prosecutor" was.

Japanese: だから私は‥‥ 検事局を去るしかなかったのだ。
English: And that is why... I had to leave the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 新しい答え‥‥正しい答えを知る までは、法廷に立てるわけがない。
English: I felt that I couldn't stand in a court of law until I knew what a prosecutor really was.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。 今度は、キミの番だ。
English: And now, Wright... It's your turn.

Japanese: ぼくの‥‥
English: My... Turn?

Japanese: キミにとっての“弁護士”には、 いったい何ができるのか‥‥？
English: What is this thing called a "lawyer"? What can you do as one?

Japanese: 答えを探し出すしかないのだよ。 ‥‥キミ自身で。
English: You must find the answer... And you must find it on your own.

Japanese: ‥‥ピピピピピピ‥‥
English: ...*beep beep beep*...

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ トランシーバーが‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Nick! The transceiver!

Japanese: ‥‥先ほどはシツレイしました。
English: I'm sorry for what happened earlier.

Japanese: どうですか？　明日こそ、 無罪判決をもらえるでしょうね？
English: Well then, Mr. Attorney. Do you wager you can obtain an acquittal tomorrow?

Japanese: おや。どうしましたか？
English: My, my. What is the matter, Mr. Attorney?

Japanese: ‥‥いつもの強気なハッタリが 感じられませんね。
English: I don't sense your usual anger this time.

Japanese: ‥‥教えてくれ。
English: ...Tell me. Please.

Japanese: なぜ、王都楼なんかのために、 真宵ちゃんを誘拐する？
English: Why are you holding Maya hostage for Mr. Engarde's sake?

Japanese: あんな‥‥人殺しのために！
English: Why are you... Why are you doing this for that cold-blooded killer!?

Japanese: 成歩堂‥‥
English: Wright...

Japanese: ゴカイなさらぬよう。 彼は、殺人を依頼しただけです。
English: Please don't misunderstand things. He is my client.

Japanese: ふざけるな！ そんなの、殺人と同じだ！
English: Don't toy with me! A man who hires an assassin is just as much of a killer himself!

Japanese: ‥‥理由をおたずねでしたね。 私がなぜ、誘拐などしたのか‥‥。
English: ...I believe you were asking me for a reason as to why I am doing what I am...

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。
English: ...Y-Yeah.

Japanese: それは‥‥いわば、 アフター・ケアでございます。
English: This is what I like to call my "aftercare".

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと？ アフター・ケア‥‥？
English: Wh-What the heck is "aftercare"?

Japanese: 私は、自分の名前に ホコリを持っております。
English: My name carries a certain amount of honor and dignity, Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: 依頼人には、ゼッタイ殺人の容疑が かからないようにする。
English: I take great care to insure that no suspicion falls upon my clients for my handiwork.

Japanese: ‥‥それが“信頼関係”であり、 殺し屋の仁義なのです。
English: That is what is called "client relations" and it is a part of an assassin's duty.

Japanese: こ‥‥“殺し屋の”‥‥？
English: An assassin's... "duty"...?

Japanese: 今回は、不幸にも依頼人が 逮捕されてしまいました。
English: We were unlucky this time and my client was arrested as a suspect.

Japanese: だから、ウデキキの弁護士さんに 働いてもらっているのですよ。
English: As a result, I did what I had to do to enlist your expert help, Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥それこそ、 死にものぐるいで‥‥。
English: And to ensure that you would do everything in your power to the very end.

Japanese: お‥‥お前‥‥お前の名前は‥‥！
English: Wh-What is your name!?

Japanese: 以前に一度、名乗ったのですが‥‥
English: I believe I told you once before, however...

Japanese: たしか‥‥
English: You did... But...

Japanese: そう。 私の名前は‥‥コロシヤです。
English: My name is... De Killer.

Japanese: コロシヤ　サザエモン。
English: Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: さ‥‥左々右エ門だと！
English: Y-You're Shelly de Killer!?

Japanese: ‥‥お忘れのないように。 あなたに選択の余地はありません。
English: Please keep in mind you do not have much space to maneuver with me.

Japanese: 虎狼死家は、シゴトは かならず、やりとげます。
English: As a De Killer, I always finish what I set out to do.

Japanese: もし、あなたがしくじれば‥‥
English: If you fail to keep up your end of the bargain...

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: 殺し屋の仁義にかけて、 彼女には死んでいただきます。
English: It would be my duty as an assassin to see to it she receives a nice long nap.

Japanese: いやああああああああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaah! Noooooooooooo!

Japanese: ‥‥では、シツレイします。
English: Now then, if you'll excuse me.

Japanese: 電波の発信源をたどられては ヤッカイですからね。
English: If someone were to trace this signal back to me, it would be quite troublesome.

Japanese: ‥‥にゃあん。
English: ...Meow.

Japanese: 真宵さま！　真宵さまあ‥‥
English: Mystic Maya! Mystic Mayaaaaa!

Japanese: な、なんということだ‥‥
English: I... I don't know what to say...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣！
English: ...Edgeworth!

Japanese: うム？
English: Hm?

Japanese: ‥‥聞いたか？ 今、通信の最後に‥‥
English: Did you hear that? At the end of that transmission...

Japanese: ‥にゃあん‥
English: ...Meow.

Japanese: あ？　ああ。あれは‥‥ ネコが鳴いていたようだな。
English: Huh? Oh, that... It sounded like a cat.
...A cat?

Japanese: （まさか‥‥あのネコ‥‥）
English: (It can't be... that cat... Can it...?)

Japanese: どうしたのですか‥‥？
English: What is it...?

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門が‥‥ 誘拐犯がいる場所がわかった！
English: I think... I know where Shelly de Killer is holding Maya hostage!

Japanese: 御剣！　警官隊を回してくれ！ ‥‥大至急、王都楼の屋敷に！
English: Edgeworth! Have all police units head for Engarde Mansion immediately!

Japanese: わかった！ キミもすぐに来るんだ！
English: Alright. You hurry over as well, then!

Japanese: 春美ちゃん！ ‥‥まだ、勝負はこれからだ！
English: Don't lose hope yet, Pearls! The fight has only just begun!

Japanese: は、はいっ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくは弁護士だ。
English: I'm a lawyer...

Japanese: でも‥‥殺人を犯した人間を 無罪にするなんて‥‥
English: But to fight for someone who is clearly a killer...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟‥‥あんな男を！
English: Matt Engarde... That man is really... Argh!

Japanese: ‥‥どんな人間だって、 正当な弁護を受けるケンリがある。
English: It doesn't matter who, every person deserves a proper defense and a fair trial.

Japanese: 当然のことだろう‥‥？
English: Isn't that the basis of our judicial system?

Japanese: “正当な弁護”‥‥ それって、なんなんだ？
English: "Proper defense"? But what exactly is that?

Japanese: ハッタリとヘリクツで ムリヤリ無罪をモギ取ることか？
English: Is it where a lawyer forcibly and blindly gets an acquittal through shouting and trickery?

Japanese: ‥‥ハッ。今さら何を言っている。
English: ...*sigh* Ironic that you of all people should say such a thing.

Japanese: キミはジッサイ、今まで そうやって戦ってきたではないか。
English: Isn't that exactly how you have fought for your clients up until now?

Japanese: え。
English: Uh...

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥たしかにそうだけど。 でも！　それは‥‥
English: W-Well, that may be true, but... But that's...

Japanese: ぼくが依頼人の無実を ココロから信じていたからだ！
English: That's because I've believed my clients to be innocent from the bottom of my heart!

Japanese: 王都楼に無罪判決を与える‥‥ そんなのは、正当な弁護じゃない！
English: But if I were to get Engarde an acquittal... That... That isn't a proper defense at all!

Japanese: “苦しんでいるヒトを 救うことができたら‥‥”
English: I became a lawyer because I thought...

Japanese: そう思って、弁護士になった。
English: I thought I could save people who were suffering and in pain...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 こんなときになって‥‥
English: But... When I look at this mess we're in...

Japanese: イチバン身近にいる彼女を、 ぼくは救うことができないんだ。
English: I can't even protect the person closest to me.

Japanese: ぼくが弁護士として戦えば‥‥ 彼女は帰ってこない。
English: Even if I win the case, I still lose in the end...

Japanese: 彼女を救うことができないんだ！
English: I just don't know what to do!

Japanese: 成歩堂。‥‥カンちがいするのも いいかげんにしろ。
English: Wright. Would you get a hold of yourself? You have it all wrong.
Huh?

Japanese: われわれはヒーローではない。 ‥‥たかが人間なのだ。
English: We aren't some sort of heroes. We're only human, you and I.

Japanese: “だれかを救う”‥‥？
English: You want to "save someone"?

Japanese: そんなことが、カンタンに できるワケ、ないだろう。
English: That's something easier said than done, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: キミは、弁護士だ。 そこから逃げることはできない。
English: You are a defense lawyer. You can't run away from that.

Japanese: キミは‥‥戦うしかないのだ。
English: You can only fight. That's all you can do.

Japanese: きみや狩魔　冥はいつでも、全力で ぼくをねじ伏せようとする。
English: People like you and Franziska von Karma are always using all you have to pin me down.

Japanese: ‥‥最後のさいごまで。 おぼろげに真相が見えたとしても。
English: You fight to the very end, even when you know the truth is not with you.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくにはムリだ。アキラカに 有罪の人物のために、
English: But I'm not like you.

Japanese: ウソの判決をダマし取るために 戦うなんて‥‥！
English: I can't fight for a false verdict -- for a man I clearly know to be guilty!

Japanese: 狩魔　冥は‥‥自分自身のために 戦っている。
English: Franziska... She fights for herself.

Japanese: “カンペキに勝利するため” ‥‥それだけのために。
English: The only thing she fights for is her perfect win record. That's all.

Japanese: それは、お前だって 同じじゃないか！
English: And!? Isn't that the same as you!?

Japanese: だから‥‥お前は逃げだしたんだ。 ‥‥１年前。
English: Isn't that why you ran away a year ago?

Japanese: お前の“勝利”がつぶされた‥‥ そんなくだらない理由で！
English: Because your precious win record was destroyed!? You are so petty!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: キミが私を憎んでいる理由は わかったような気がする。
English: Now I understand why you despise me so.

Japanese: しかし‥‥それは まちがっている。
English: However, you are mistaken.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんだって‥‥？
English: What do you...?

Japanese: キミによって、カンペキな 勝利への道をとざされたとき‥‥
English: Thanks to you, when you sealed off my path to a perfect win record...

Japanese: 私は、自分のあやまちに 気づき始めたのだ。
English: I began to realize the error of my ways.

Japanese: “カンペキな勝利”など、 なんのイミもない。
English: I realized that things such as a perfect record were meaningless.

Japanese: え‥‥っ！
English: Eh!?

Japanese: ウソだ。‥‥それなら、 なぜ検事局から消えた？
English: I don't believe you. Are you saying that is why you left the Prosecutor's Office?

Japanese: そして‥‥ どうして今、ここにいる？
English: But then, why? Why are you here now?

Japanese: その答えは‥‥
English: The answer to that... is something you will find out on your own.

Japanese: キミ自身で見つけだすんだな。 ‥‥明日、判決が出るまでに。
English: I have faith you will see it before the verdict is read tomorrow.

Japanese: その答えがわからなければ‥‥ 結末を変えることはできない。
English: But if you can't, then you will be powerless to change the ending of this story.

Japanese: （今は‥‥法廷記録のことを 話している場合じゃないな‥‥）
English: (Now's not the time to talk about evidence...)

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥逃げられたのか）
English: (We've searched all over, but it looks like he got away...)

Japanese: ザンネンだが‥‥
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: エモノはすでに、 飛び去ったようだ。
English: It looks like he slipped out of our grasp this time.

Japanese: これで‥‥また、 手がかりを失ったわけか。
English: And now... we've lost our only lead.

Japanese: 希望を捨てるな。 ‥‥あの子が、キミを見ている。
English: Don't give up yet. That little girl is looking to you to be her pillar.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、そうだな。
English: ...Yeah, you're right.

Japanese: すでに、手は打ってある。
English: We're close, I can tell.

Japanese: 街から出るすべてのルートに 検問を配備した。
English: We've already set up check- points along every route leading out of this district.

Japanese: ‥‥あとは、われわれのシゴトだ。
English: Leave the rest to us.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: これは‥‥ 由利恵さんの写真‥‥だな。
English: This looks like a picture of Ms. Inpax...

Japanese: （“愛をこめて‥‥ユリエ”）
English: ("With love... Celeste")

Japanese: 由利恵さんというのは、 たしか‥‥
English: Ms. Inpax? You mean...?

Japanese: イサオさんのマネージャーだ。
English: Yes, Mr. Corrida's former manager.

Japanese: どうしてそんな方の写真が 王都楼さんの屋敷に‥‥？
English: Why would a picture of Ms. Inpax be here in Mr. Engarde's mansion?

Japanese: （“愛をこめて”‥‥か。 どうやら、つながったな‥‥）
English: (And why does it say, "With love"...? Hmm, this might be a clue...)

Japanese: 証拠品《由利恵の写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Celeste's Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: どうしたの、春美ちゃん。
English: What's wrong, Pearls?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっとその写真立て、 貸してください！
English: P-Please let me see that picture frame!

Japanese: （なんだ‥‥？　写真立ての ウラを見ているけど‥‥）
English: (Huh? What's so special about the frame?)

Japanese: ウラに‥‥写真立てのウラに、 何か書いてあります！
English: On the back... There's something written on the back of the frame!
..."Maya"

Japanese: 真宵さまからです！ 真宵さまが‥‥お手紙を！
English: It's Mystic Maya! She left us a message!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: やっぱり来てくれたね。 あたし、信じてたんだよ。
English: I thought you'd come. I knew you would.

Japanese: いい？　王都楼のコト‥‥ かならず有罪にしてやってよね！
English: Now listen up! You'd better get Engarde a guilty sentence, OK!?

Japanese: あんなヒドいヤツを無罪にしたら ‥‥あたし、ユルさないから！
English: If you get that creepy slimebag a not guilty, I'll never forgive you, ever!

Japanese: あたしのことなら、だいじょうぶ。 シンパイ、いらないから。
English: I'm fine, so you don't need to worry.

Japanese: ‥‥書きたいこと、いっぱい あるけど‥‥時間がないみたい。
English: ...There's so much I want to write, but I don't think I have a lot of time left.

Japanese: はみちゃん。そこにいるよね？
English: Pearly, you're there too, right?

Japanese: なるほどくんを助けてあげてね。 まだまだ‥‥たよりないから。
English: Make sure you help Nick, OK? Someone's gotta watch out for the helpless lunk.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥またね、なるほどくん。 さよなら。
English: Um... That's it for now, Nick. I guess I'll talk to you guys later.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんな‥‥イヤ。 ‥‥イヤです！
English: ...That's... I... Nooooooooo!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまあ‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Mystic Mayaaaaaaa... *sob*

Japanese: 成歩堂！
English: Wright!

Japanese: どうした？　ボンヤリして。
English: What's wrong? Why the blank stare?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥なんでもない。
English: Oh, um, nothing.

Japanese: ここが最後の部屋だ。 ‥‥やはり‥‥逃げられたか。
English: We've searched the house, and this is the last room. It looks like he eluded us.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。
English: ...Edgeworth.

Japanese: なんだ？
English: Yes?

Japanese: 事件の手がかりは、 これでだいたい、そろった。
English: As far as clues go, I think this is about all I'm going to get.

Japanese: しかし‥‥最後に１つ。 足りないものがある。
English: But I'm still short one last thing...

Japanese: ‥‥それは？
English: And what is that?

Japanese: （霧緒さんのサイコ・ロック‥‥）
English: (Ms. Andrews' Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: （彼女のヒミツを知ることが できれば‥‥）
English: (If I could just find out what secret she's holding...)

Japanese: （ぼくは明日、法廷に 立つことができる）
English: (Then I think I stand a chance in court tomorrow...)

Japanese: （‥‥真実を暴くために‥‥）
English: (To blow this case wide open and expose the truth.)

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろう。 留置所には連絡を入れておく。
English: I think I know what you're thinking. I'll contact the detention center.

Japanese: ああ‥‥すまないな、御剣。
English: Um... Thanks, Edgeworth.

Japanese: さあ、行くよ。春美ちゃん。 ‥‥最後のカギを開けるんだ。
English: Well, let's go Pearls. It's time to open that last lock.

Japanese: 明かりがとどかないため、 よく見えない。
English: The light doesn't filter in all the way, so it's hard to see.

Japanese: 木箱やらダンボールやら ‥‥ハリガネでしばられている。
English: It looks like there are crates and cardboard boxes tied up with wire here.

Japanese: わあ‥‥
English: Wow...

Japanese: わたくし、こんなたくさんの ビンを見るの、初めてです。
English: I've never seen so many bottles all in one place before.

Japanese: カネなら、うなるほど あるからね。スターは‥‥。
English: Well, I guess when you're a star, you have so much money you can buy anything you want.

Japanese: ずいぶん重いドアですね。 わたくし、開けられません。
English: It's a pretty heavy door, isn't it? I can't open it by myself.

Japanese: 置いていかないでくださいね、 なるほどくん。
English: Please don't shut me in here by accident, OK Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （こんなところに 閉じこめられたら‥‥）
English: (If I were locked in a place like this...)

Japanese: （ぼくもコワいな）
English: (I'd be plenty scared too. Trust me.)

Japanese: くそッ！ だれもいないか‥‥
English: Argh! There's no one here!

Japanese: ここは‥‥どうやら、 王都楼の私室らしいな。
English: From the looks of this room, I would say this is Engarde's private lounge.

Japanese: ‥‥見ろ、成歩堂！
English: ...Look at this, Wright!

Japanese: 電波を受信するアンテナと ビデオデッキがある‥‥
English: An antennae for sending and receiving radio signals and a VCR...

Japanese: デッキの中を見てくれ！ ビデオテープは、重要な証拠だ！
English: Check inside the deck! If there's a tape, it would be an important piece of evidence!

Japanese: （なにしろ、犯行の瞬間が 記録されているんだからな！）
English: (If we're lucky, it'll have the moment the crime was committed recorded on it!)

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥
English: I'm sorry, but...

Japanese: ビデオデッキの中はカラだ。 テープは入っていない。
English: The tape deck is empty. There's no tape to be found.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: No...

Japanese: しかし、コイツを使って 録画していたのは、まちがいない。
English: But there's no mistake that someone used this to record something.

Japanese: おそらく‥‥だれかが テープを持ち去ったのだろう‥‥
English: It looks like someone took the tape we're looking for, and escaped with it...

Japanese: 巨大なテレビとスピーカーが 立ちはだかっている。
English: A huge television and speakers loom largely here.

Japanese: きっと、真宵ちゃんが見たら‥‥
English: I'm sure if Maya saw this, she'd say,

Japanese: 『こんなテレビでトノサマンが 見られたら、死んでもいいや』
English: "I would die a happy Samurai fan if I could see the Nickel Samurai on a TV like this!"

Japanese: ‥‥とか、言いそうだな。
English: Yeah, that's what she'd say...

Japanese: いやいやいや！ シャレにならないな、今のは。
English: Ack! I can't believe I just made a joke about Maya, all things considered...

Japanese: ビデオデッキと‥‥アンテナ。
English: It's a VCR and an antennae.

Japanese: 現場にしかけられた、 盗撮カメラの映像は‥‥
English: The footage that the spy camera took at the scene of the crime...

Japanese: ここで受信して、 録画されていたんだ。
English: was beamed here and recorded on tape.

Japanese: せめて、テープがあれば よかったのですが‥‥。
English: If only we had that tape, it would have been really helpful...

Japanese: 王都楼のパソコン‥‥か。
English: It's Engarde's computer.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、コイツを使って 助けを求めればよかったのに‥‥
English: Maya... Why couldn't you have used this to get help?

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: M-Mr. Nick!

Japanese: スイッチは‥‥ 電源スイッチはどこですかっ！
English: Where's... Where's the power switch!?

Japanese: （そういうことか‥‥）
English: (Oh, I get it... So that's what happened. She couldn't find the power switch...)

Japanese: シュウちゃんのための 小さなドアがついています。
English: There's a little door for Shoe in this big door.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、真宵ちゃんは ここからコイツを‥‥。
English: I'm sure Maya used this door to shove this out there... I'm sure it was her!

Japanese: （きみがくれる証拠は、 いつだって最強の切り札だった）
English: (Every time you give me some piece of evidence, it's always the most crucial piece.)

Japanese: （きっと、今回も‥‥）
English: (This time too, I'm sure...)

Japanese: 暗くてよく見えないけど‥‥ ビデオテープのコレクションだ。
English: I can't read all the labels, but this is a very large collection of video tapes.

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼が、自分の番組を 録画していたらしい。
English: Looks like Engarde taped all of his own shows...

Japanese: ゆったりしたソファだ。
English: What a spacious sofa.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんなら １０人ぐらい座れそうだ。
English: I bet 10 Pearls would fit on here.

Japanese: １０人はムリだと 思いますけど‥‥。
English: Um, I don't think 10 of me would fit, Mr. Nick...

Japanese: とても小さなテーブルだ。
English: It's a very small table.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが３人も 座れば、いっぱいだろう。
English: I bet we could barely squeeze 3 Pearls on there.

Japanese: わたくし、テーブルになんて 座りませんけど‥‥。
English: But I wouldn't sit on a table to begin with, Mr. Nick...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼邸　応接間
English: March 22 Engarde Mansion Living Room

Japanese: ‥‥だれもいないね。
English: It looks like no one is around...

Japanese: あの、ぬいぐるみのような カオをした方は‥‥？
English: Um, what happened to that person with the stuffed teddy face...?

Japanese: （‥‥ああ、ハデに ぬい目があった執事さんか‥‥）
English: (Oh, she must mean that butler with the stitches in his face.)

Japanese: シュウちゃあん。
English: Shoooooe!

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: あら。まだ起きていたのですね、 シュウちゃん。
English: Oh, there you are. I guess you're still awake, huh Shoe?

Japanese: にゃあん。
English: Meow.

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。おねえさんが 遊んであげますね。
English: Hee hee, come on, let's play.

Japanese: （執事の田中さんは‥‥ もう、寝ちゃったのかな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if that butler, Mr. Doe, is already asleep or not...)

Japanese: きょっ、巨大な自転車が 空を飛んでいます！
English: A... A giant bicycle is flying through the air!

Japanese: あの自転車はね。 こがなくても走るんだよ。
English: That bicycle, Pearls, is one where you don't have to pedal, and it moves on its own.

Japanese: ええッ！　すごい！
English: Really!? Wow!

Japanese: でもね。 ザンネンながら、飛ばないんだ。
English: But sorry to disappoint you, it can't fly.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。ガッカリです。
English: ...Oh. That's too bad.

Japanese: あ。大きなカマドがあります。
English: Oh! There's a giant cooking hearth here.

Japanese: それはダンロだよ。
English: That's actually a fireplace.

Japanese: どうちがうのですか？
English: How are they different, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （そういえば、カマドって 見たことないな、ぼく‥‥）
English: (You know, I've never actually seen a hearth before, come to think of it...)

Japanese: 綾里のお屋敷に、遊びに 来てください。お見せしますから。
English: You should come and visit Fey Manor, then. I'll show you one when you do.

Japanese: ゆったりとした 応接セットだ。
English: It's a very comfortable and spacious lounge set.

Japanese: 有名スターたちが 笑いさざめくのだろうか。
English: I wonder if famous stars drop by and sit around and have a good time.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ、ぼくの 居場所なんか、ないだろうな。
English: ...In any case, I don't really belong here, do I?

Japanese: なんだ？ ‥‥このヒガミ根性は。
English: Argh. What is with me and feeling inferior today...?

Japanese: あ。お面があります！
English: Ah! There are masks here!

Japanese: ああ。まんなかのマスクが トノサマンだよ。
English: Yeah. That one in the middle is the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: あとは、ヒメサマンと、 アクダイカーン。
English: The ones next to that are the Pink Princess and the Evil Magistrate.

Japanese: ネオ・エドシティを舞台に、 いつも戦っているんだ。
English: They fought many battles against the backdrop of Neo Olde Tokyo.

Japanese: はあ‥‥なるほどくん、 お詳しいのですね。
English: Wow, you really know a lot about the Steel Samurai, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: （子どもより詳しいと、 なんか恥ずかしいな‥‥）
English: (I don't know whether to laugh or cry that I know more about that show than a kid...)

Japanese: ドアの下に、ちっちゃな ドアがついています。
English: There's a small door at the bottom of this bigger door, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ネコが出入りするための ものじゃないかな。
English: I bet it's for Mr. Engarde's cat to use.

Japanese: ああ。シュウちゃん。
English: Oh, you mean Shoe.

Japanese: （ドアには‥‥ カギがかかっている）
English: (The door... It's locked tight.)

Japanese: （まあ、勝手に出入りしちゃ マズいよな‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess that's to keep nosey people like me from entering it.)

Japanese: あそこにもドアがある。
English: There's another door over there.

Japanese: 行っちゃダメですよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You shouldn't go wandering off over there, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes, Pearls.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのときと、 立場が逆だな‥‥）
English: (Now I know how Maya feels when I tell her to stop playing around...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 王都楼邸　応接間
English: March 22 Engarde Mansion Living Room

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: へんじをしてください！ 真宵さまあ！
English: Please answer us! Mystic Mayaaaa!

Japanese: このエリアは、完全に包囲した。 逃げることは不可能だ。
English: We have this area completely surrounded. There is no way for him to escape.

Japanese: まだ、このあたりに いれば、のハナシだが‥‥。
English: Assuming he's still in the area...

Japanese: まさか‥‥あの執事が 虎狼死家だったなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe it... That butler... All this time, he was De Killer...

Japanese: なんといっても、彼らは 共犯関係にあるわけだからな。
English: He and Engarde were working together all this time.

Japanese: 万が一の場合にそなえて、 打ち合わせをしておいたのだろう。
English: I'm sure they had worked out a contingency plan ahead of time.

Japanese: あ。これ‥‥クマさんの アクセサリーですよ！
English: Oh, it's a figurine of a bear!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、切れ目が いっぱい入っていますけど。
English: But there are a lot of cuts in it for some reason...

Japanese: 証拠品《アクセサリー》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Figurine added to the Court Record.

Japanese: “クマ”‥‥といえば、 イサオさんだけど。
English: A bear...? Isn't that more of a thing for Mr. Corrida?

Japanese: どうしてこんなところに 置いてあるんだ‥‥？
English: Why would something like this be here...?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。ドアの下を見ろ。 ネコ用の入り口がついている。
English: Wright, look down. There's a little pet door installed here.

Japanese: あ‥‥ きっと、シュウちゃんの‥‥。
English: Ah, I'm sure that's for Shoe...

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥コイツは、 その小さな入り口から‥‥？
English: Do you think that this... came through that little door...?

Japanese: うム‥‥！　このドア‥‥ カギがかかっているぞ！
English: Umph! This door... It's locked!

Japanese: ドアをコワすのは慣れているよ。 ‥‥行くぞ、御剣。
English: Well, I'm pretty used to breaking doors down by now. Let's go, Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥ドカッ！　‥‥ドカッ！ バァン！
English: *SLAM* *SLAM* *CRASH!*

Japanese: 王都楼は、バイクのレーサーと しても、かなり有名らしい。
English: It seems that Engarde was quite famous, even as a motorcycle racer.

Japanese: タレントがそういうコトをすると、 たいていシッパイするけど。
English: Generally when a star like him tries to do something like that, they fail, but...

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼はやはり、 タダモノではない、ということだ。
English: It seems Engarde is no ordinary man.

Japanese: ホンモノのダンロなんて、 初めて見るな。
English: This is the first time I've seen such a large fireplace.

Japanese: ‥‥ダンロは好きではない。 少年時代を思い出すから。
English: I don't particularly care for large fireplaces. They remind me of my youth.

Japanese: （コイツ‥‥カネ持ちの おぼっちゃんだったからな）
English: (I wonder, if memory serves, if it's because of that rich aunt he had?)

Japanese: お前、まだ あの事件のことを‥‥？
English: Don't tell me you're still stuck on that incident...?

Japanese: キミには悪いが‥‥
English: Don't take this personally,

Japanese: 思い出したくないんだ。 あのころのコトは。
English: but I really do not wish to remember anything from that time in my life.

Japanese: 王都楼のヤツ‥‥
English: That Engarde...

Japanese: ここで、取り引きをしたのかも しれないな‥‥虎狼死家と。
English: This is probably where he conducted business with De Killer.
Yeah...

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門は、 依頼人との信頼関係を重んじる。
English: Shelly de Killer values his client relations more than anything else.

Japanese: かならず本人と会って シゴトの話をするらしい。
English: So I'm sure he must meet with his clients in person to talk about any transactions.

Japanese: 王都楼のコレクションだ。
English: It's Engarde's personal collection.

Japanese: 自分に関係のある番組の 小道具を集めるのがシュミ、とか。
English: I'd say it's a hobby of his to collect prop masks of shows related to his own.

Japanese: ‥‥お前、イヤに詳しいな。 王都楼　真悟のコト。
English: You seem to know all sorts of details about Matt Engarde... Almost too many details...

Japanese: 事件の被告人だ。 当然のコトだろう。
English: He's the defendant in this case. Of course I would know about him.

Japanese: そ‥‥そりゃそうだな、うん。
English: Y-Yeah... I guess that makes sense...

Japanese: ドアの下に、ちっちゃな ドアがついています。
English: There's a small door at the bottom of this bigger door, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ネコが出入りするための ものじゃないかな。
English: I bet it's for the cat to use.

Japanese: ああ。シュウちゃん。
English: Oh, you mean Shoe.

Japanese: 奥に部屋があるみたいだな。
English: It looks like there's another room in the back.

Japanese: あっちは われわれに任せろ。
English: Leave that to us.

Japanese: 警官隊がすでに 調査にかかっているはずだ。
English: I'm sure the investigation team is already doing its job in there.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
